1952:1.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic is a state of the working people of towns and villages.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic is a state of the working people of towns and villages.

1952:2.contentEn: The basis of popular power in the Romanian People's Republic is the alliance of the working class with the working peasantry, in which the leading role belongs to the working class.

content.text: The basis of popular power in the Romanian People's Republic is the alliance of the working class with the working peasantry, in which the leading role belongs to the working class.

1952:3.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic was born and strengthened as a result of the liberation of the country by the armed forces of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics from under the yoke of fascism and from under imperialist domination, as a result of the overthrow of the power of the landlords and capitalists by the masses of towns and villages led by the working class, under the leadership of the Romanian Communist Party.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic was born and strengthened as a result of the liberation of the country by the armed forces of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics from under the yoke of fascism and from under imperialist domination, as a result of the overthrow of the power of the landlords and capitalists by the masses of towns and villages led by the working class, under the leadership of the Romanian Communist Party.

1952:4.contentEn:
In the Romanian People's Republic, power belongs to the working people of towns and villages, who exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and the People's Councils.
The People's Councils constitute the political basis of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, power belongs to the working people of towns and villages, who exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and the People's Councils.
The People's Councils constitute the political basis of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:5.contentEn: The national economy of the Romanian People's Republic comprises three socio-economic formations: the socialist formation, small-scale commodity production, and the private-capitalist formation.

content.text: The national economy of the Romanian People's Republic comprises three socio-economic formations: the socialist formation, small-scale commodity production, and the private-capitalist formation.

1952:6.contentEn:
The foundation of the socialist socio-economic formation is socialist ownership of the means of production, which takes either the form of state property (common property of the people) or the form of cooperative-collectivist property (property of collective agricultural holdings or cooperative organizations).
In the socialist formation of the national economy, the exploitation of man by man is liquidated.
The socialist formation, to which the leading role in the national economy of the Romanian People's Republic belongs, constitutes the basis for the country's development on the path of socialism. The state of people's democracy, proclaiming that its main task is the construction of socialism, strengthens and expands the socialist formation unceasingly, ensures the uninterrupted increase of the material well-being and cultural level of the working people.

content.text: The foundation of the socialist socio-economic formation is socialist ownership of the means of production, which takes either the form of state property (common property of the people) or the form of cooperative-collectivist property (property of collective agricultural holdings or cooperative organizations).
In the socialist formation of the national economy, the exploitation of man by man is liquidated.

The socialist formation, to which the leading role in the national economy of the Romanian People's Republic belongs, constitutes the basis for the country's development on the path of socialism. The state of people's democracy, proclaiming that its main task is the construction of socialism, strengthens and expands the socialist formation unceasingly, ensures the uninterrupted increase of the material well-being and cultural level of the working people.

1952:7.contentEn: The riches of any nature of the subsoil, factories, plants and mines, forests, waters, sources of natural energy, means of communication of any kind, railway, river, maritime and air transport, banks, post office, telegraph, telephone, radio, printing facilities, cinematography and theatre, state agricultural holdings, machine and tractor stations, communal enterprises and the nationalized part of the housing stock in towns, constitute state property, common property of the people.

content.text: The riches of any nature of the subsoil, factories, plants and mines, forests, waters, sources of natural energy, means of communication of any kind, railway, river, maritime and air transport, banks, post office, telegraph, telephone, radio, printing facilities, cinematography and theatre, state agricultural holdings, machine and tractor stations, communal enterprises and the nationalized part of the housing stock in towns, constitute state property, common property of the people.

1952:8.contentEn: The land in the Romanian People's Republic belongs to those who cultivate it.

content.text: The land in the Romanian People's Republic belongs to those who cultivate it.

1952:9.contentEn:
The live and dead inventory of collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives, the production achieved, as well as all enterprises or constructions belonging to them, represent the common property of the collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives.
The peasants who are members of the collective agricultural holdings have for their personal use a plot of land next to the house and in personal ownership the household on this plot, the dwelling house, productive animals, poultry, small agricultural inventory – in accordance with the statute of the collective agricultural holding.

content.text: The live and dead inventory of collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives, the production achieved, as well as all enterprises or constructions belonging to them, represent the common property of the collective agricultural holdings and cooperatives.

The peasants who are members of the collective agricultural holdings have for their personal use a plot of land next to the house and in personal ownership the household on this plot, the dwelling house, productive animals, poultry, small agricultural inventory – in accordance with the statute of the collective agricultural holding.

1952:10.contentEn:
Small commodity production in the Romanian People's Republic includes small and medium-sized peasant households, which possess private property over the land based on the producer's own labor, as well as the workshops of craftsmen who do not exploit the labor of others. The State protects the right of private property over the land of peasants with small and medium-sized households, based on the laws in force.
The people's democratic State supports the peasants with small and medium-sized households and the craftsmen, with the aim of protecting them from capitalist exploitation, of increasing the production achieved by them and of raising their welfare.

content.text: Small commodity production in the Romanian People's Republic includes small and medium-sized peasant households, which possess private property over the land based on the producer's own labor, as well as the workshops of craftsmen who do not exploit the labor of others. The State protects the right of private property over the land of peasants with small and medium-sized households, based on the laws in force.

The people's democratic State supports the peasants with small and medium-sized households and the craftsmen, with the aim of protecting them from capitalist exploitation, of increasing the production achieved by them and of raising their welfare.

1952:11.contentEn:
The private-capitalist formation in the Romanian People's Republic includes kulak households, private commercial enterprises, small non-nationalized industrial enterprises, based on the exploitation of wage labor.
The people's democratic State consistently implements the policy of restricting and eliminating capitalist elements.

content.text: The private-capitalist formation in the Romanian People's Republic includes kulak households, private commercial enterprises, small non-nationalized industrial enterprises, based on the exploitation of wage labor.
The people's democratic State consistently implements the policy of restricting and eliminating capitalist elements.

1952:12.contentEn: The right of personal property of the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic over income and savings derived from labor, over the dwelling house and auxiliary household next to the house, over household and personal use items, as well as the right of inheritance over the personal property of citizens are protected by law.

content.text: The right of personal property of the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic over income and savings derived from labor, over the dwelling house and auxiliary household next to the house, over household and personal use items, as well as the right of inheritance over the personal property of citizens are protected by law.

1952:13.contentEn: The economic life of the Romanian People's Republic develops on the basis of the state plan of the national economy, in the interest of building socialism, the continuous increase of the material and cultural well-being of the working people, the strengthening of the country's national independence and its defense capacity.

content.text: The economic life of the Romanian People's Republic develops on the basis of the state plan of the national economy, in the interest of building socialism, the continuous increase of the material and cultural well-being of the working people, the strengthening of the country's national independence and its defense capacity.

1952:14.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, foreign trade is a state monopoly.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, foreign trade is a state monopoly.

1952:15.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, work is a duty and a matter of honor for every citizen capable of work, according to the principle “whoever does not work, does not eat”. In the Romanian People's Republic, the principle of socialism is being implemented more and more widely: “From each according to his abilities, to each according to his work”.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, work is a duty and a matter of honor for every citizen capable of work, according to the principle “whoever does not work, does not eat”. In the Romanian People's Republic, the principle of socialism is being implemented more and more widely: “From each according to his abilities, to each according to his work”.

1952:16.contentEn: The state regime of the Romanian People's Republic is the regime of people's democracy, which represents the power of the working people.

content.text: The state regime of the Romanian People's Republic is the regime of people's democracy, which represents the power of the working people.
The Romanian People's Democratic State – a unitary, sovereign, and independent state:

a) defends the independence and sovereignty of the Romanian people, the gains of the working people in towns and villages, the rights, freedoms, and power of the working people, against the enemies of the working people;

b) ensures the strengthening and development of the country's productive forces through its socialist industrialization, through the elimination of economic, technical, and cultural backwardness, through the gradual socialist transformation of agriculture based on the free consent of the working peasants;
c) organizes and develops the planned economy, relying on state and cooperative enterprises;
d) organizes the defense of the Republic against external enemies and commands the country's armed forces;
e) ensures the internal security of citizens, renders harmless and represses the enemies of the people;
f) manages the monetary and credit system, elaborates and implements the state budget, establishes the taxes, fees, and revenues necessary for the needs of the state;
g) administers the state industrial, agricultural, and commercial banks, enterprises, and institutions;
h) manages public education at all levels;
i) ensures the continuous improvement of the well-being and health of the popular masses in towns and villages;
j) ensures the development of the Romanian people's culture and the culture of national minorities, socialist in content, national in form;
k) ensures the application and observance of the Constitution and laws of the Romanian People's Republic, as they are the expression of the will and interests of the working people.

The observance and strict application of the Constitution and laws of the country is the main duty of every state institution and every citizen, as they are binding throughout the territory of the Republic.

1952:18.contentEn:
The Romanian People's Republic has the following administrative-territorial division:
The Regions: Arad, Bacău, Baia Mare, Bîrlad, Bucharest, Cluj, Constanța, Craiova, Galați, Hunedoara, Iași, Oradea, Pitești, Ploiești, Stalin, Suceava, Timișoara, the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic has the following administrative-territorial division:
The Regions: Arad, Bacău, Baia Mare, Bîrlad, Bucharest, Cluj, Constanța, Craiova, Galați, Hunedoara, Iași, Oradea, Pitești, Ploiești, Stalin, Suceava, Timișoara, the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

1952:19.contentEn:
The Hungarian Autonomous Region of the Romanian People's Republic is formed from the territory inhabited by the compact Hungarian Szekler population and has autonomous administrative leadership, elected by the population of the Autonomous Region.
The Hungarian Autonomous Region includes the districts: Ciuc, Gheorgheni, Odorhei, Reghin, Sîngeorgiu de Pădure, Sf. Gheorghe, Tîrgu-Mureș, Tîrgu-Secuiesc, Toplița.
The administrative center of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is the city of Tîrgu-Mureș.

content.text: The Hungarian Autonomous Region of the Romanian People's Republic is formed from the territory inhabited by the compact Hungarian Szekler population and has autonomous administrative leadership, elected by the population of the Autonomous Region.
The Hungarian Autonomous Region includes the districts: Ciuc, Gheorgheni, Odorhei, Reghin, Sîngeorgiu de Pădure, Sf. Gheorghe, Tîrgu-Mureș, Tîrgu-Secuiesc, Toplița.
The administrative center of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is the city of Tîrgu-Mureș.

1952:20.contentEn: The laws of the Romanian People's Republic, the decisions and provisions of the central state bodies are binding on the territory of the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

content.text: The laws of the Romanian People's Republic, the decisions and provisions of the central state bodies are binding on the territory of the Hungarian Autonomous Region.

1952:21.contentEn: The Regulation of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is elaborated by the People's Council of the Autonomous Region and submitted for approval to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Regulation of the Hungarian Autonomous Region is elaborated by the People's Council of the Autonomous Region and submitted for approval to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:22.contentEn: The supreme body of state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The supreme body of state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:23.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly is the sole legislative body of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly is the sole legislative body of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Grand National Assembly has in its direct competence:
a) the election of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic;
b) the formation of the Government of the Romanian People's Republic;
c) the amendment of the Constitution;
d) matters of war and peace;
e) the establishment of national economy plans;
f) the approval of the state budget, the conclusion of budget exercises, and the establishment of taxes and revenues intended for the state budget;
g) the establishment of the number of ministries, the naming, merging, and dissolution of ministries;
h) the modification of the division of the territory of the Romanian People's Republic into regions;
i) the granting of amnesty;
j) general control over the application of the Constitution.

1952:25.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly is elected by the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, in electoral districts, one deputy for every 40,000 inhabitants.
The Grand National Assembly is elected for a term of 4 years.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly is elected by the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, in electoral districts, one deputy for every 40,000 inhabitants.
The Grand National Assembly is elected for a term of 4 years.

1952:26.contentEn: A law is deemed adopted if it is voted for by a simple majority of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: A law is deemed adopted if it is voted for by a simple majority of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:27.contentEn:
After the adoption of laws by the Grand National Assembly, they are signed by the President and the Secretary of the Presidium and published in the Official Bulletin of the Grand National Assembly.
The fulfillment of the laws adopted by the Grand National Assembly is mandatory for all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: After the adoption of laws by the Grand National Assembly, they are signed by the President and the Secretary of the Presidium and published in the Official Bulletin of the Grand National Assembly.
The fulfillment of the laws adopted by the Grand National Assembly is mandatory for all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:28.contentEn: The sessions of the Grand National Assembly are held twice a year. The convocation of the Grand National Assembly is done by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The sessions of the Grand National Assembly are held twice a year. The convocation of the Grand National Assembly is done by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:29.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly may be convened in extraordinary sessions by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, or at the request of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly may be convened in extraordinary sessions by the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, or at the request of at least one third of the total number of deputies.

1952:30.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly elects in each legislature a President and two Vice-Presidents, who conduct the meetings of the Grand National Assembly in accordance with the internal rules of procedure.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly elects in each legislature a President and two Vice-Presidents, who conduct the meetings of the Grand National Assembly in accordance with the internal rules of procedure.

1952:31.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly validates the mandates of the elected deputies.
In order to verify the conditions under which the election of each deputy took place, the Grand National Assembly elects a validation committee. This committee submits its report for the approval of the Grand National Assembly, which decides on the validation or annulment of the election of each deputy.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly validates the mandates of the elected deputies.

In order to verify the conditions under which the election of each deputy took place, the Grand National Assembly elects a validation committee. This committee submits its report for the approval of the Grand National Assembly, which decides on the validation or annulment of the election of each deputy.

1952:32.contentEn:
The Grand National Assembly may appoint committees of inquiry and review on various issues.
All state bodies and officials are obliged to provide these committees with the necessary information and documents.
The Grand National Assembly establishes for each committee separately its powers and mode of activity.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly may appoint committees of inquiry and review on various issues.
All state bodies and officials are obliged to provide these committees with the necessary information and documents.
The Grand National Assembly establishes for each committee separately its powers and mode of activity.

1952:33.contentEn: Each deputy has the right to ask questions or to interpellate the Government or individual ministers. The Government or the minister questioned has the obligation to respond verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days.

content.text: Each deputy has the right to ask questions or to interpellate the Government or individual ministers. The Government or the minister questioned has the obligation to respond verbally or in writing, within a maximum of 3 days.

1952:34.contentEn: No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be brought to trial or arrested without the approval of the Grand National Assembly during sessions, and between sessions – of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: No deputy of the Grand National Assembly may be brought to trial or arrested without the approval of the Grand National Assembly during sessions, and between sessions – of the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:35.contentEn: The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic elects the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, composed of a President, two Vice-Presidents, a Secretary and thirteen members.

content.text: The Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic elects the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly, composed of a President, two Vice-Presidents, a Secretary and thirteen members.

1952:36.contentEn: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly is responsible for all its activity to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly is responsible for all its activity to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Presidium of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic has the following attributions:
a) convenes the Grand National Assembly in sessions;
b) issues decrees;
c) interprets the laws in force in the Romanian People's Republic;
d) decides on consulting the people (referendum);
e) annuls the decisions and provisions of the Council of Ministers when they do not conform to the laws;

f) in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly, dismisses and appoints ministers to the Government at the proposal of the President of the Council of Ministers and with the subsequent approval of the Grand National Assembly;
g) establishes the decorations, medals and honorary titles of the Romanian People's Republic;
h) confers the decorations, medals and honorary titles of the Romanian People's Republic; establishes military ranks, diplomatic ranks and other special titles;

i) in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, at the proposal of the Government, declares a state of war in the event of armed aggression directed against the Romanian People's Republic or against another state, in respect of which the Romanian People's Republic has obligations of mutual defense arising from international treaties;
j) appoints and dismisses the supreme commander of the armed forces of the Romanian People's Republic;
k) declares partial or general mobilization;
l) exercises the right of pardon and commutation of sentences;
m) ratifies and denounces the international treaties of the Romanian People's Republic;
n) accredits and recalls the plenipotentiary representatives of the Romanian People's Republic to foreign states;
o) receives the letters of accreditation and recall of the diplomatic representatives of foreign states, accredited to the Presidium;

p) in the interest of defending the Romanian People's Republic or ensuring public order and state security, proclaims a state of emergency in some localities or throughout the territory of the country.

1952:38.contentEn: The mandate of the Grand National Assembly ceases upon the expiration of the term for which it was elected.

content.text: The mandate of the Grand National Assembly ceases upon the expiration of the term for which it was elected.

1952:39.contentEn:
After the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly sets new elections within a maximum of three months, counted from the day of the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly.
The Presidium in office retains its powers until the election of the new Presidium by the new Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: After the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly sets new elections within a maximum of three months, counted from the day of the expiration of the mandate of the Grand National Assembly.
The Presidium in office retains its powers until the election of the new Presidium by the new Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:40.contentEn: In the event of war or other exceptional circumstances, the Grand National Assembly may extend its mandate for the duration of the exceptional situation.

content.text: In the event of war or other exceptional circumstances, the Grand National Assembly may extend its mandate for the duration of the exceptional situation.

1952:41.contentEn: The newly elected Grand National Assembly shall be convened by the acting Presidium within a maximum of three months after the elections.

content.text: The newly elected Grand National Assembly shall be convened by the acting Presidium within a maximum of three months after the elections.

1952:42.contentEn: The supreme executive and dispositive body of the state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The supreme executive and dispositive body of the state power of the Romanian People's Republic is the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Council of Ministers is constituted by the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic and is composed of:
– The President of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic;
– The Vice-Presidents of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic;
– The President of the State Planning Committee;
– The President of the State Control Commission;
– The Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic;
– The President of the State Supply Committee;
– The President of the State Committee for the Collection of Agricultural Products;
– The President of the Higher Education Committee;
– The President of the Cinematography Committee;
– The President of the Art Committee.

1952:44.contentEn: The Council of Ministers is responsible and accountable for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly – to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The Council of Ministers is responsible and accountable for its activity to the Grand National Assembly, and in the interval between the sessions of the Grand National Assembly – to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly.

1952:45.contentEn: The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic issues decisions and orders based on the laws in force or for the purpose of their application and controls their execution.

content.text: The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic issues decisions and orders based on the laws in force or for the purpose of their application and controls their execution.

1952:46.contentEn: The application of the decisions and orders of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic is mandatory throughout the entire territory of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The application of the decisions and orders of the Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic is mandatory throughout the entire territory of the Romanian People's Republic.
The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic has the following attributions:
a) coordinates and guides the activity of the ministries and other institutions subordinated to it;
b) takes measures for the realization of the national economy plan, the state budget and for the consolidation of the monetary and credit system;
c) takes measures to ensure public order, defend the interests of the state and protect the rights of citizens;
d) exercises general leadership in the field of relations with foreign states;
e) establishes the annual quotas of citizens who are to be called to perform active military service; leads the general organization of the armed forces of the country;
f) establishes, as necessary, special committees and commissions, as well as general directorates within the Council of Ministers for economic, cultural, legal and military issues.

1952:48.contentEn: The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic may annul the orders and instructions of ministers that do not conform to the laws and decisions of the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The Council of Ministers of the Romanian People's Republic may annul the orders and instructions of ministers that do not conform to the laws and decisions of the Council of Ministers.

1952:49.contentEn: Within the limits of the competence of the ministries they lead, the Ministers issue orders and instructions based on and for the purpose of applying the laws in force, the decisions and provisions of the Council of Ministers, controlling their execution.

content.text: Within the limits of the competence of the ministries they lead, the Ministers issue orders and instructions based on and for the purpose of applying the laws in force, the decisions and provisions of the Council of Ministers, controlling their execution.
The Ministries of the Romanian People's Republic are the following:
- Ministry of Foreign Affairs;
- Ministry of Internal Affairs;
- Ministry of Agriculture;
- Ministry of Foreign Trade;
- Ministry of Internal Trade;
- Ministry of Constructions and the Construction Materials Industry;
- Ministry of Religious Affairs;
- Ministry of Electric Energy and Electrotechnical Industry;
- Ministry of Finance;
- Ministry of the Armed Forces;
- Ministry of State Agricultural Enterprises;
- Ministry of Communal Management and Local Industry;
- Ministry of Forestry Management;
- Ministry of Food Industry;
- Ministry of Meat, Fish and Milk Industry;
- Ministry of Coal Industry;
- Ministry of Chemical Industry;
- Ministry of Metallurgical Industry;
- Ministry of Petroleum Industry;
- Ministry of Wood, Paper and Cellulose Industry;
- Ministry of Light Industry;
- Ministry of Public Education;
- Ministry of Justice;
- Ministry of Post and Telecommunications;
- Ministry of Social Security;
- Ministry of Health;
- Ministry of State Security;
- Ministry of Transports;

1952:51.contentEn: The bodies of state power in regions, districts, cities and communes are the People's Councils of the working people from cities and villages.

content.text: The bodies of state power in regions, districts, cities and communes are the People's Councils of the working people from cities and villages.

1952:52.contentEn:
The People's Councils are composed of deputies elected for a term of two years by the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, from the respective region, district, city and commune.
The norms of representation in the People's Councils are established by law.

content.text: The People's Councils are composed of deputies elected for a term of two years by the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, from the respective region, district, city and commune.
The norms of representation in the People's Councils are established by law.

1952:53.contentEn: The People's Councils guide the work of the administrative bodies subordinate to them, conduct local activity in the economic and cultural field, ensure the maintenance of public order, respect for the laws and the protection of citizens' rights, and draw up the local budget.

content.text: The People's Councils guide the work of the administrative bodies subordinate to them, conduct local activity in the economic and cultural field, ensure the maintenance of public order, respect for the laws and the protection of citizens' rights, and draw up the local budget.

1952:54.contentEn: The People's Councils organize the active participation of the working people in the management of state and public affairs and in the work of building socialism.

content.text: The People's Councils organize the active participation of the working people in the management of state and public affairs and in the work of building socialism.

1952:55.contentEn: The People's Councils take decisions and issue provisions within the limits of the rights granted to them by the laws of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The People's Councils take decisions and issue provisions within the limits of the rights granted to them by the laws of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:56.contentEn: The executive and disposal bodies of the regional, district, city and communal People's Councils are the Executive Committees elected by the deputies of the People's Councils and are composed of the President, Vice-Presidents, Secretary and members.

content.text: The executive and disposal bodies of the regional, district, city and communal People's Councils are the Executive Committees elected by the deputies of the People's Councils and are composed of the President, Vice-Presidents, Secretary and members.

1952:57.contentEn:
The body of state power of the Magyar Autonomous Region is the People's Council of the Autonomous Region.
The executive and administrative body of the People's Council of the Magyar Autonomous Region is the Executive Committee elected by it.

content.text: The body of state power of the Magyar Autonomous Region is the People's Council of the Autonomous Region.
The executive and administrative body of the People's Council of the Magyar Autonomous Region is the Executive Committee elected by it.

1952:58.contentEn: The People's Council of the Magyar Autonomous Region is elected according to the norms established by law, for a term of 2 years, by the working people of the Autonomous Region, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The People's Council of the Magyar Autonomous Region is elected according to the norms established by law, for a term of 2 years, by the working people of the Autonomous Region, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:59.contentEn: The executive and administrative body of the People's Councils in small communes is composed of the President, Vice-President and Secretary, elected by the deputies of the respective People's Council.

content.text: The executive and administrative body of the People's Councils in small communes is composed of the President, Vice-President and Secretary, elected by the deputies of the respective People's Council.

1952:60.contentEn: The executive and administrative bodies of the People's Councils are accountable for their activity both to the People's Council that elected them and to the Executive Committee of the immediately superior People's Council.

content.text: The executive and administrative bodies of the People's Councils are accountable for their activity both to the People's Council that elected them and to the Executive Committee of the immediately superior People's Council.

1952:61.contentEn: The regional, district and city People's Councils organize sections of the Executive Committees. The organization, mode of operation and activity of the sections are determined by law.

content.text: The regional, district and city People's Councils organize sections of the Executive Committees. The organization, mode of operation and activity of the sections are determined by law.

1952:62.contentEn: The sections of the Executive Committees of the People's Councils are subordinate both to the People's Council and the Executive Committee alongside which they operate, and to the corresponding section of the Executive Committee of the immediately superior People's Council and the respective Ministries.

content.text: The sections of the Executive Committees of the People's Councils are subordinate both to the People's Council and the Executive Committee alongside which they operate, and to the corresponding section of the Executive Committee of the immediately superior People's Council and the respective Ministries.

1952:63.contentEn: Upon the expiration of the mandate of the People's Councils, the Executive Committees retain their powers until the constitution of the new executive bodies by the newly elected People's Councils.

content.text: Upon the expiration of the mandate of the People's Councils, the Executive Committees retain their powers until the constitution of the new executive bodies by the newly elected People's Councils.

1952:64.contentEn:
Justice in the Romanian People's Republic is administered by the Supreme Tribunal of the Romanian People's Republic, the regional Tribunals and the people's Tribunals, as well as by the special judicial instances, established by law.
The organization, competence and procedure of the tribunals are established by law.

content.text: Justice in the Romanian People's Republic is administered by the Supreme Tribunal of the Romanian People's Republic, the regional Tribunals and the people's Tribunals, as well as by the special judicial instances, established by law.
The organization, competence and procedure of the tribunals are established by law.

1952:65.contentEn: The courts defend the regime of people's democracy and the gains of the working people; they ensure people's legality, public property and the rights of citizens.

content.text: The courts defend the regime of people's democracy and the gains of the working people; they ensure people's legality, public property and the rights of citizens.

1952:66.contentEn: Trials in all courts shall be conducted with the participation of people's assessors, except where the law provides otherwise.

content.text: Trials in all courts shall be conducted with the participation of people's assessors, except where the law provides otherwise.

1952:67.contentEn:
The Supreme Court of the Romanian People's Republic is elected by the Grand National Assembly for a term of five years.
Judges and people's assessors are elected in accordance with the procedure established by law.
The appointment of judges in special courts is also established by law.

content.text: The Supreme Court of the Romanian People's Republic is elected by the Grand National Assembly for a term of five years.
Judges and people's assessors are elected in accordance with the procedure established by law.
The appointment of judges in special courts is also established by law.

1952:68.contentEn:
In the Romanian People's Republic, judicial procedure shall be conducted in Romanian, ensuring in regions and districts inhabited by a population of a nationality other than Romanian the use of the mother tongue of that population.
The parties, who do not speak the language in which the judicial procedure is conducted, shall be ensured the possibility to become acquainted, through an interpreter, with the documents in the file, as well as the right to speak in court and to present conclusions in their mother tongue.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, judicial procedure shall be conducted in Romanian, ensuring in regions and districts inhabited by a population of a nationality other than Romanian the use of the mother tongue of that population.

The parties, who do not speak the language in which the judicial procedure is conducted, shall be ensured the possibility to become acquainted, through an interpreter, with the documents in the file, as well as the right to speak in court and to present conclusions in their mother tongue.

1952:69.contentEn:
In all courts, the trial is public, except in cases provided for by law.
The accused is guaranteed the right of defence.

content.text: In all courts, the trial is public, except in cases provided for by law.
The accused is guaranteed the right of defence.

1952:70.contentEn: Judges are independent and subject only to the law.

content.text: Judges are independent and subject only to the law.

1952:71.contentEn: The courts pronounce their judgments in the name of the people.

content.text: The courts pronounce their judgments in the name of the people.

1952:72.contentEn: The Supreme Court of the Romanian People's Republic exercises supervision of the judicial activity of all courts in the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Supreme Court of the Romanian People's Republic exercises supervision of the judicial activity of all courts in the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:73.contentEn: The Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic shall exercise supreme supervision over the observance of the laws by the Ministries and other central bodies, by the local bodies of state power and administration, as well as by officials and other citizens.

content.text: The Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic shall exercise supreme supervision over the observance of the laws by the Ministries and other central bodies, by the local bodies of state power and administration, as well as by officials and other citizens.

1952:74.contentEn: The Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic shall be appointed by the Grand National Assembly for a term of five years. The deputies of the Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic and the prosecutors of the local units of the Prosecutor's Office shall be appointed by the Prosecutor General for a term of four years.

content.text: The Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic shall be appointed by the Grand National Assembly for a term of five years. The deputies of the Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic and the prosecutors of the local units of the Prosecutor's Office shall be appointed by the Prosecutor General for a term of four years.

1952:75.contentEn: The Prosecutor General shall be accountable to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, and – in the interval between sessions – to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly and to the Council of Ministers.

content.text: The Prosecutor General shall be accountable to the Grand National Assembly of the Romanian People's Republic, and – in the interval between sessions – to the Presidium of the Grand National Assembly and to the Council of Ministers.

1952:76.contentEn: The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office shall be independent of the local bodies, being subordinated only to the Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The bodies of the Prosecutor's Office shall be independent of the local bodies, being subordinated only to the Prosecutor General of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:77.contentEn:
The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall be guaranteed the right to work, that is, the right to obtain guaranteed work and paid according to its quantity and quality.
The right to work shall be guaranteed by the existence and development of the socialist formation of the national economy, by the uninterrupted and systematic increase of the forces of production in the Romanian People's Republic, by the elimination of the possibility of economic crises and by the liquidation of unemployment.

content.text: The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall be guaranteed the right to work, that is, the right to obtain guaranteed work and paid according to its quantity and quality.

The right to work shall be guaranteed by the existence and development of the socialist formation of the national economy, by the uninterrupted and systematic increase of the forces of production in the Romanian People's Republic, by the elimination of the possibility of economic crises and by the liquidation of unemployment.
The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to rest.

The right to rest shall be ensured by: the establishment of an 8-hour working day for workers and employees; the reduction of the working day to less than 8 hours for certain professions with difficult working conditions and for sections with particularly difficult working conditions;
the establishment of paid annual holidays for all workers and employees; making available to the working people rest homes, sanatoriums and cultural institutions.

1952:79.contentEn:
The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to material security in old age, in case of illness or incapacity for work.
This right shall be guaranteed by the broad development of social insurance for workers and employees, at the expense of the state, by free medical assistance granted to those who work and by making available to the working people balneary and climatic resorts.

content.text: The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to material security in old age, in case of illness or incapacity for work.

This right shall be guaranteed by the broad development of social insurance for workers and employees, at the expense of the state, by free medical assistance granted to those who work and by making available to the working people balneary and climatic resorts.

1952:80.contentEn:
The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to education.
This right shall be ensured by general, compulsory and free elementary education; by the system of state scholarships granted to meritorious students and pupils from schools of higher, secondary and elementary education, by the organization, alongside industrial enterprises, state agricultural holdings, machine and tractor stations and collective agricultural holdings, of free vocational education for those who work.
Education of all levels shall be state education.
The state shall take care of the development of science, literature and art.

content.text: The citizens of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to education.

This right shall be ensured by general, compulsory and free elementary education; by the system of state scholarships granted to meritorious students and pupils from schools of higher, secondary and elementary education, by the organization, alongside industrial enterprises, state agricultural holdings, machine and tractor stations and collective agricultural holdings, of free vocational education for those who work.
Education of all levels shall be state education.
The state shall take care of the development of science, literature and art.

1952:81.contentEn:
Working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, regardless of nationality or race, are ensured full equality of rights in all fields of economic, political and cultural life.
Any direct or indirect restriction of the rights of working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, the establishment of direct or indirect privileges based on the race or nationality to which the citizens belong, any manifestation of chauvinism, racial hatred, national hatred or chauvinistic nationalist propaganda is punished by law.

content.text: Working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, regardless of nationality or race, are ensured full equality of rights in all fields of economic, political and cultural life.

Any direct or indirect restriction of the rights of working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, the establishment of direct or indirect privileges based on the race or nationality to which the citizens belong, any manifestation of chauvinism, racial hatred, national hatred or chauvinistic nationalist propaganda is punished by law.

1952:82.contentEn: In the Romanian People's Republic, national minorities are ensured the free use of their mother tongue, education of all levels in their mother tongue, books, newspapers and theaters in their mother tongue. In the districts inhabited also by populations of a nationality other than Romanian, all bodies and institutions shall use orally and in writing also the language of the respective nationalities and shall make appointments of officials from among the respective nationality or other locals who know the language and way of life of the local population.

content.text: In the Romanian People's Republic, national minorities are ensured the free use of their mother tongue, education of all levels in their mother tongue, books, newspapers and theaters in their mother tongue. In the districts inhabited also by populations of a nationality other than Romanian, all bodies and institutions shall use orally and in writing also the language of the respective nationalities and shall make appointments of officials from among the respective nationality or other locals who know the language and way of life of the local population.

1952:83.contentEn:
Women in the Romanian People's Republic have equal rights with men in all fields of economic, political, state and cultural life.
Women have equal rights with men to work, salary, rest, social insurance and education.
The state protects marriage and the family and defends the interests of the mother and child. The state provides assistance to mothers with many children and single mothers, paid leave for pregnant women; organizes maternity hospitals, nurseries and children's homes.

content.text: Women in the Romanian People's Republic have equal rights with men in all fields of economic, political, state and cultural life.
Women have equal rights with men to work, salary, rest, social insurance and education.

The state protects marriage and the family and defends the interests of the mother and child. The state provides assistance to mothers with many children and single mothers, paid leave for pregnant women; organizes maternity hospitals, nurseries and children's homes.

1952:84.contentEn:
Freedom of conscience is guaranteed to all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.
Religious denominations are free to organize and may function freely. The freedom to exercise religious denominations is guaranteed to all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.
The school is separated from the church. No religious denomination, congregation or community may open or maintain general education institutions, but only special schools for the training of cult personnel.
The manner of organization and functioning of religious denominations is regulated by law.

content.text: Freedom of conscience is guaranteed to all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.
Religious denominations are free to organize and may function freely. The freedom to exercise religious denominations is guaranteed to all citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

The school is separated from the church. No religious denomination, congregation or community may open or maintain general education institutions, but only special schools for the training of cult personnel.
The manner of organization and functioning of religious denominations is regulated by law.
In accordance with the interests of those who work and in order to strengthen the regime of people's democracy, the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic are guaranteed by law:
a) freedom of speech;
b) freedom of the press;
c) freedom of assembly and meetings;
d) freedom of processions and street demonstrations.

These rights are ensured by placing at the disposal of the working masses and their organizations the printing houses, paper depots, public buildings, streets, means of communication and other material conditions necessary for the exercise of these rights.

1952:86.contentEn:
In accordance with the interests of those who work and for the purpose of developing the political and public activity of the popular masses, the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic are ensured the right to associate in public organizations, in professional trade unions, cooperative unions, women's, youth, sports organizations, cultural, technical and scientific associations.
Any association of a fascist or anti-democratic nature is prohibited. Participation in such associations is punished by law.
The most active and most conscious citizens from the ranks of the working class and from the ranks of other strata of working people unite in the Romanian Workers' Party, the vanguard detachment of the working people in the struggle for strengthening and developing the regime of people's democracy and for building the socialist society.
The Romanian Workers' Party is the leading force of both the organizations of those who work and the state bodies and institutions. All the organizations of those who work in the Romanian People's Republic gather around it.

content.text: In accordance with the interests of those who work and for the purpose of developing the political and public activity of the popular masses, the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic are ensured the right to associate in public organizations, in professional trade unions, cooperative unions, women's, youth, sports organizations, cultural, technical and scientific associations.
Any association of a fascist or anti-democratic nature is prohibited. Participation in such associations is punished by law.

The most active and most conscious citizens from the ranks of the working class and from the ranks of other strata of working people unite in the Romanian Workers' Party, the vanguard detachment of the working people in the struggle for strengthening and developing the regime of people's democracy and for building the socialist society.

The Romanian Workers' Party is the leading force of both the organizations of those who work and the state bodies and institutions. All the organizations of those who work in the Romanian People's Republic gather around it.

1952:87.contentEn:
The inviolability of the person is guaranteed to the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.
No one may be arrested except on the basis of a court or prosecutor's decision, in accordance with the provisions of the law.

content.text: The inviolability of the person is guaranteed to the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.
No one may be arrested except on the basis of a court or prosecutor's decision, in accordance with the provisions of the law.

1952:88.contentEn: The inviolability of the domicile of citizens and the secrecy of correspondence are protected by law.

content.text: The inviolability of the domicile of citizens and the secrecy of correspondence are protected by law.

1952:89.contentEn: The Romanian People's Republic grants the right of asylum to foreign citizens persecuted for defending the interests of the working people, for scientific activity, for participation in the national liberation struggle or in the defense of peace.

content.text: The Romanian People's Republic grants the right of asylum to foreign citizens persecuted for defending the interests of the working people, for scientific activity, for participation in the national liberation struggle or in the defense of peace.

1952:90.contentEn: Every citizen of the Romanian People's Republic is bound to observe the Constitution and the laws of the State of people's democracy; to preserve, strengthen and develop socialist public property; to observe labor discipline; to actively contribute to the strengthening of the regime of people's democracy and to the economic and cultural advancement of the country.

content.text: Every citizen of the Romanian People's Republic is bound to observe the Constitution and the laws of the State of people's democracy; to preserve, strengthen and develop socialist public property; to observe labor discipline; to actively contribute to the strengthening of the regime of people's democracy and to the economic and cultural advancement of the country.

1952:91.contentEn: Military service is compulsory. Military service in the ranks of the Armed Forces of the Romanian People's Republic is a duty of honor of the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: Military service is compulsory. Military service in the ranks of the Armed Forces of the Romanian People's Republic is a duty of honor of the citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:92.contentEn: The defense of the Homeland is the sacred duty of every citizen of the Romanian People's Republic. Treason against the Homeland, violation of the oath, defection to the enemy, prejudice to the defense capacity of the state, espionage constitute the most serious crimes against the people and the state and are punished by law with all severity.

content.text: The defense of the Homeland is the sacred duty of every citizen of the Romanian People's Republic. Treason against the Homeland, violation of the oath, defection to the enemy, prejudice to the defense capacity of the state, espionage constitute the most serious crimes against the people and the state and are punished by law with all severity.

1952:93.contentEn: The elections of deputies for the Grand National Assembly and for the People's Councils are held by universal, equal, direct and secret ballot.

content.text: The elections of deputies for the Grand National Assembly and for the People's Councils are held by universal, equal, direct and secret ballot.

1952:94.contentEn:
The elections of deputies are held by universal suffrage. All working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, who have reached the age of 18, regardless of race or nationality, sex, religion, level of culture, profession or length of domicile, have the right to take part in the election of deputies, with the exception of the alienated, persons convicted by court decision to the loss of electoral rights and those declared unworthy by law.
Any working person, a citizen of the Romanian People's Republic with the right to vote, who has reached the age of 23, may be elected as a deputy of the Grand National Assembly and of the People's Councils.

content.text: The elections of deputies are held by universal suffrage. All working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, who have reached the age of 18, regardless of race or nationality, sex, religion, level of culture, profession or length of domicile, have the right to take part in the election of deputies, with the exception of the alienated, persons convicted by court decision to the loss of electoral rights and those declared unworthy by law.

Any working person, a citizen of the Romanian People's Republic with the right to vote, who has reached the age of 23, may be elected as a deputy of the Grand National Assembly and of the People's Councils.

1952:95.contentEn: The elections of deputies are held by equal vote. All working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, take part in the elections on equal terms and each has the right to a single vote.

content.text: The elections of deputies are held by equal vote. All working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, take part in the elections on equal terms and each has the right to a single vote.

1952:96.contentEn: Women shall have the right to elect and to be elected to the Grand National Assembly and to the People's Councils on an equal footing with men.

content.text: Women shall have the right to elect and to be elected to the Grand National Assembly and to the People's Councils on an equal footing with men.

1952:97.contentEn: Citizens serving in the Armed Forces of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to elect and to be elected, like all working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: Citizens serving in the Armed Forces of the Romanian People's Republic shall have the right to elect and to be elected, like all working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:98.contentEn: The election of deputies shall be by direct vote. In the elections for the Grand National Assembly and for all People's Councils, the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, shall participate directly, by direct vote.

content.text: The election of deputies shall be by direct vote. In the elections for the Grand National Assembly and for all People's Councils, the working people, citizens of the Romanian People's Republic, shall participate directly, by direct vote.

1952:99.contentEn: The election of deputies shall be by secret ballot.

content.text: The election of deputies shall be by secret ballot.

1952:100.contentEn: Candidacies for the election of deputies shall be submitted by electoral districts, according to the norms established by law. The right to submit candidacies shall be ensured to all organizations of the working people: organizations of the Romanian Workers' Party, professional trade unions, cooperatives, youth organizations and other mass organizations, as well as cultural associations.

content.text: Candidacies for the election of deputies shall be submitted by electoral districts, according to the norms established by law. The right to submit candidacies shall be ensured to all organizations of the working people: organizations of the Romanian Workers' Party, professional trade unions, cooperatives, youth organizations and other mass organizations, as well as cultural associations.

1952:101.contentEn:
Each deputy shall be obliged to give account to the voters of his activity and of that of the elected body of which he is a member.
At any time, the deputy's mandate may be withdrawn, following the decision of the majority of the voters, taken in accordance with the procedure established by law.

content.text: Each deputy shall be obliged to give account to the voters of his activity and of that of the elected body of which he is a member.
At any time, the deputy's mandate may be withdrawn, following the decision of the majority of the voters, taken in accordance with the procedure established by law.

1952:102.contentEn: The Coat of Arms of the Romanian People's Republic represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. On the left side of the Coat of Arms there is a drilling rig. The Coat of Arms is framed by a wreath of wheat ears. At the top of the Coat of Arms there is a five-pointed star. At the bottom of the Coat of Arms, the ears are wrapped in a tricolor ribbon on which the letters R.P.R. are written.

content.text: The Coat of Arms of the Romanian People's Republic represents wooded mountains, above which the sun rises. On the left side of the Coat of Arms there is a drilling rig. The Coat of Arms is framed by a wreath of wheat ears. At the top of the Coat of Arms there is a five-pointed star. At the bottom of the Coat of Arms, the ears are wrapped in a tricolor ribbon on which the letters R.P.R. are written.

1952:103.contentEn: The Flag of the Romanian People's Republic bears the colors red, yellow and blue, placed vertically with the blue next to the lance. In the middle is placed the Coat of Arms of the Romanian People's Republic.

content.text: The Flag of the Romanian People's Republic bears the colors red, yellow and blue, placed vertically with the blue next to the lance. In the middle is placed the Coat of Arms of the Romanian People's Republic.

1952:104.contentEn: The capital of the Romanian People's Republic is the city of Bucharest.

content.text: The capital of the Romanian People's Republic is the city of Bucharest.

1952:105.contentEn: The Constitution of the Romanian People's Republic may be amended only by a law voted by the Grand National Assembly.

content.text: The Constitution of the Romanian People's Republic may be amended only by a law voted by the Grand National Assembly.

1986:1.contentEn: Romania is a socialist republic. The Socialist Republic of Romania is a state of the working people from cities and villages, sovereign, independent and unitary. Its territory is inalienable and indivisible.

content.text: Romania is a socialist republic. The Socialist Republic of Romania is a state of the working people from cities and villages, sovereign, independent and unitary. Its territory is inalienable and indivisible.

1986:2.contentEn:
All power in the Socialist Republic of Romania belongs to the people, free and in control of their destiny.
People's power is based on the worker-peasant alliance. In close unity, the working class – the leading class in society -, the peasantry, the intelligentsia, the other categories of working people, regardless of nationality, build the socialist order, creating the conditions for the transition to communism.

content.text: All power in the Socialist Republic of Romania belongs to the people, free and in control of their destiny.

People's power is based on the worker-peasant alliance. In close unity, the working class – the leading class in society -, the peasantry, the intelligentsia, the other categories of working people, regardless of nationality, build the socialist order, creating the conditions for the transition to communism.

1986:3.contentEn: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the leading political force of the entire society is the Romanian Communist Party.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the leading political force of the entire society is the Romanian Communist Party.

1986:4.contentEn:
As the sovereign holder of power, the people exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and through the people's councils, bodies elected by universal, equal, direct and secret vote.
The Grand National Assembly and the people's councils constitute the basis of the entire system of state bodies.
The Grand National Assembly is the supreme body of state power, under whose leadership and control all other state bodies carry out their activity.

content.text: As the sovereign holder of power, the people exercise it through the Grand National Assembly and through the people's councils, bodies elected by universal, equal, direct and secret vote.
The Grand National Assembly and the people's councils constitute the basis of the entire system of state bodies.
The Grand National Assembly is the supreme body of state power, under whose leadership and control all other state bodies carry out their activity.

1986:5.contentEn:
The national economy of Romania is a socialist economy, based on socialist ownership of the means of production.
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the exploitation of man by man is forever abolished, and the socialist principle of distribution according to the quantity and quality of work is implemented.
Work is a duty of honor for every citizen of the country.

content.text: The national economy of Romania is a socialist economy, based on socialist ownership of the means of production.
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the exploitation of man by man is forever abolished, and the socialist principle of distribution according to the quantity and quality of work is implemented.
Work is a duty of honor for every citizen of the country.

1986:6.contentEn: Socialist ownership of the means of production is either state property – over goods belonging to the entire people, or cooperative property – over goods belonging to each cooperative organization.

content.text: Socialist ownership of the means of production is either state property – over goods belonging to the entire people, or cooperative property – over goods belonging to each cooperative organization.

1986:7.contentEn: Resources of any nature in the subsoil, mines, lands from the state land fund, forests, waters, natural energy sources, factories and plants, banks, state agricultural enterprises, stations for the mechanization of agriculture, communication routes, state means of transport and telecommunications, the state fund of buildings and dwellings, the material basis of state socio-cultural institutions, belong to the entire people, are state property.

content.text: Resources of any nature in the subsoil, mines, lands from the state land fund, forests, waters, natural energy sources, factories and plants, banks, state agricultural enterprises, stations for the mechanization of agriculture, communication routes, state means of transport and telecommunications, the state fund of buildings and dwellings, the material basis of state socio-cultural institutions, belong to the entire people, are state property.

1986:8.contentEn: Foreign trade is a state monopoly.

content.text: Foreign trade is a state monopoly.

1986:9.contentEn:
The land of agricultural production cooperatives, the animals, tools, installations and constructions belonging to them are cooperative property.
The plot of land which, according to the statute of the agricultural production cooperatives, is used by the family households of the cooperative peasants, constitutes cooperative property.
The dwelling house and the household constructions, the land on which they are located, as well as, according to the statute of the agricultural cooperatives, the production animals and the small agricultural inventory, constitute the personal property of the cooperative peasants. The tools, machines, installations and constructions of handicraft cooperatives and of cooperatives for the production, acquisition and sale of goods are cooperative property.

content.text: The land of agricultural production cooperatives, the animals, tools, installations and constructions belonging to them are cooperative property.
The plot of land which, according to the statute of the agricultural production cooperatives, is used by the family households of the cooperative peasants, constitutes cooperative property.

The dwelling house and the household constructions, the land on which they are located, as well as, according to the statute of the agricultural cooperatives, the production animals and the small agricultural inventory, constitute the personal property of the cooperative peasants. The tools, machines, installations and constructions of handicraft cooperatives and of cooperatives for the production, acquisition and sale of goods are cooperative property.

1986:10.contentEn:
Agricultural production cooperatives, a socialist form of organization of agriculture, ensure conditions for the intensive cultivation of the land and the application of advanced science, contribute, through increased production, to the development of the national economy, to the continuous raising of the living standards of the peasantry and of the entire people.
The state supports agricultural production cooperatives and protects their property. The state also supports other cooperative organizations and protects their property.

content.text: Agricultural production cooperatives, a socialist form of organization of agriculture, ensure conditions for the intensive cultivation of the land and the application of advanced science, contribute, through increased production, to the development of the national economy, to the continuous raising of the living standards of the peasantry and of the entire people.
The state supports agricultural production cooperatives and protects their property. The state also supports other cooperative organizations and protects their property.

1986:11.contentEn:
Under the conditions of cooperativized agriculture, the state guarantees to the peasants who cannot associate in agricultural production cooperatives the ownership of the land they work themselves and their families, of the tools used for this purpose, as well as the ownership of working and production animals.
The ownership of craftsmen over their own workshops is also guaranteed.

content.text: Under the conditions of cooperativized agriculture, the state guarantees to the peasants who cannot associate in agricultural production cooperatives the ownership of the land they work themselves and their families, of the tools used for this purpose, as well as the ownership of working and production animals.
The ownership of craftsmen over their own workshops is also guaranteed.

1986:12.contentEn: Land and buildings may be expropriated only for works of public interest and with the payment of fair compensation.

content.text: Land and buildings may be expropriated only for works of public interest and with the payment of fair compensation.

In the Socialist Republic of Romania, the entire state activity is aimed at developing the order and flourishing of the socialist nation, the continuous increase of the material and cultural well-being of the people, ensuring the freedom and dignity of man, the multilateral affirmation of the human personality.
For this, the Romanian socialist state:
Organizes, plans and manages the national economy;
Protects socialist property;
Guarantees the full exercise of the rights of citizens, ensures socialist legality and defends the rule of law;
Develops education of all levels, ensures the conditions for the development of science, art and culture, carries out health protection;
Ensures the defense of the country and organizes its armed forces;
Organizes relations with other states.

1986:14.contentEn:
The Socialist Republic of Romania maintains and develops relations of friendship and collaboration with the socialist countries in the spirit of socialist internationalism, promotes relations of collaboration with countries having a different social-political order, is active in international organizations in order to ensure peace and understanding between peoples.
The external relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania are based on the principles of respect for national sovereignty and independence, equality of rights and mutual advantage, non-interference in internal affairs.

content.text: The Socialist Republic of Romania maintains and develops relations of friendship and collaboration with the socialist countries in the spirit of socialist internationalism, promotes relations of collaboration with countries having a different social-political order, is active in international organizations in order to ensure peace and understanding between peoples.

The external relations of the Socialist Republic of Romania are based on the principles of respect for national sovereignty and independence, equality of rights and mutual advantage, non-interference in internal affairs.

1986:15.contentEn:
The territory of the Socialist Republic of Romania is organised into administrative-territorial units: the county, the city and the commune. The capital of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the municipality of Bucharest, which is organised into sectors.
The more important cities may be organised as municipalities.

content.text: The territory of the Socialist Republic of Romania is organised into administrative-territorial units: the county, the city and the commune. The capital of the Socialist Republic of Romania is the municipality of Bucharest, which is organised into sectors.
The more important cities may be organised as municipalities.

1986:16.contentEn: Romanian citizenship is acquired and lost according to the law.

content.text: Romanian citizenship is acquired and lost according to the law.

1986:17.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania, without distinction as to nationality, race, sex or religion, are equal in rights in all fields of economic, political, legal, social and cultural life.
The State guarantees the equality of rights of citizens. No restriction of these rights and no distinction in their exercise on the grounds of nationality, race, sex or religion shall be permitted.
Any manifestation aimed at establishing such restrictions, nationalist-chauvinist propaganda, incitement to racial or national hatred, shall be punished by law.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania, without distinction as to nationality, race, sex or religion, are equal in rights in all fields of economic, political, legal, social and cultural life.
The State guarantees the equality of rights of citizens. No restriction of these rights and no distinction in their exercise on the grounds of nationality, race, sex or religion shall be permitted.
Any manifestation aimed at establishing such restrictions, nationalist-chauvinist propaganda, incitement to racial or national hatred, shall be punished by law.

1986:18.contentEn:
In the Socialist Republic of Romania, citizens have the right to work. Every citizen is ensured the possibility to carry out, according to his/her training, an activity in the economic, administrative, social or cultural field, remunerated according to its quantity and quality. Equal pay for equal work.
The law shall establish measures for the protection and safety of labour, as well as special measures for the protection of the labour of women and youth.

content.text: In the Socialist Republic of Romania, citizens have the right to work. Every citizen is ensured the possibility to carry out, according to his/her training, an activity in the economic, administrative, social or cultural field, remunerated according to its quantity and quality. Equal pay for equal work.
The law shall establish measures for the protection and safety of labour, as well as special measures for the protection of the labour of women and youth.

1986:19.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to rest.
The right to rest is guaranteed to those who work by establishing the maximum duration of the working day at 8 hours, a weekly rest and paid annual leave.
In heavy and very heavy work sectors, the duration of the working day is reduced below 8 hours, without reducing remuneration.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to rest.
The right to rest is guaranteed to those who work by establishing the maximum duration of the working day at 8 hours, a weekly rest and paid annual leave.
In heavy and very heavy work sectors, the duration of the working day is reduced below 8 hours, without reducing remuneration.

1986:20.contentEn:
Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to material security in old age, illness or incapacity for work.
The right to material security is realised for workers and civil servants through pensions and sickness benefits granted within the state social insurance system, and for members of cooperative organisations or other public organisations, through the forms of insurance organised by them. The State provides medical assistance through its health institutions.
Paid maternity leave is guaranteed.

content.text: Citizens of the Socialist Republic of Romania have the right to material security in old age, illness or incapacity for work.
