content.text: Meetings, demonstrations, processions or any other assemblies are free and may be organized and conducted only peacefully, without any kind of weapons.

2003:40.contentEn:
1. Citizens may freely associate in political parties, trade unions, employers' associations, and other forms of association.
2. Parties or organizations which, by their purposes or activity, militate against political pluralism, the principles of the rule of law, or the sovereignty, integrity, or independence of Romania are unconstitutional.
3. Judges of the Constitutional Court, the ombudsmen, magistrates, active members of the army, policemen, and other categories of public servants established by organic law may not be members of political parties.
4. Associations of a secret nature are prohibited.

content.text: Citizens may freely associate in political parties, trade unions, employers' associations, and other forms of association.

Parties or organizations which, by their purposes or activity, militate against political pluralism, the principles of the rule of law, or the sovereignty, integrity, or independence of Romania are unconstitutional.

Judges of the Constitutional Court, the ombudsmen, magistrates, active members of the army, policemen, and other categories of public servants established by organic law may not be members of political parties.
Associations of a secret nature are prohibited.

2003:41.titleEn: Labour and Social Protection of Labour
2003:41.contentEn:
1. The right to work may not be restricted. The free choice of profession, occupation, or trade, as well as of the workplace, is guaranteed.
2. Employees have the right to social protection measures. These concern the safety and health of employees, the working conditions of women and young people, the establishment of a minimum gross salary per country, weekly rest, paid holidays, work performed under special or specific conditions, vocational training, as well as other specific situations, established by law.
3. The normal duration of a working day is, on average, a maximum of 8 hours.
4. For equal work, women shall receive equal pay with men.
5. The right to collective bargaining in matters of labour and the binding nature of collective agreements are guaranteed.

content.text: The right to work may not be restricted. The free choice of profession, occupation, or trade, as well as of the workplace, is guaranteed.

Employees have the right to social protection measures. These concern the safety and health of employees, the working conditions of women and young people, the establishment of a minimum gross salary per country, weekly rest, paid holidays, work performed under special or specific conditions, vocational training, as well as other specific situations, established by law.
The normal duration of a working day is, on average, a maximum of 8 hours.
For equal work, women shall receive equal pay with men.
The right to collective bargaining in matters of labour and the binding nature of collective agreements are guaranteed.
Forced labour is prohibited.
The following shall not constitute forced labour:
a) activities for the fulfillment of military duties, as well as those carried out, according to the law, in their place, for religious or conscientious reasons;
b) the work of a convicted person, performed under normal conditions, during the period of detention or conditional release;
c) services required in the event of calamities or other danger, as well as those that are part of the normal civil obligations established by law.

2003:43.contentEn:
1. Employees have the right to strike in order to defend their professional, economic, and social interests.
2. The law shall establish the conditions and limits for the exercise of this right, as well as the guarantees necessary to ensure essential services for society.

content.text: Employees have the right to strike in order to defend their professional, economic, and social interests.
The law shall establish the conditions and limits for the exercise of this right, as well as the guarantees necessary to ensure essential services for society.

2003:44.contentEn:
1. The right of property, as well as debts owed by the State, are guaranteed. The content and limitations of these rights are established by law.
2. Private property is guaranteed and equally protected by law, irrespective of its holder. Foreign citizens and stateless persons may acquire the right of private ownership of land only under the conditions resulting from Romania's accession to the European Union and from other international treaties to which Romania is a party, on a reciprocal basis, under the conditions provided by organic law, as well as by legal inheritance.
3. No one may be expropriated except for a cause of public utility, established according to law, with just and prior compensation.
4. Nationalization or any other measures of forced transfer of assets to public ownership based on the social, ethnic, religious, political or other discriminatory affiliation of the holders are prohibited.
5. For works of general interest, the public authority may use the subsoil of any immovable property, with the obligation to compensate the owner for damages caused to the soil, plantations or constructions, as well as for other damages imputable to the authority.
6. The compensations provided for in paragraphs (3) and (5) shall be established by mutual agreement with the owner or, in case of disagreement, by a court of law.
7. The right of property obliges to the observance of duties regarding environmental protection and ensuring good neighborliness, as well as to the observance of other duties which, according to law or custom, fall upon the owner.
8. Assets lawfully acquired may not be confiscated. The lawful nature of the acquisition is presumed.
9. Goods intended for, used in or resulting from offenses or contraventions may be confiscated only under the conditions provided by law.

content.text: The right of property, as well as debts owed by the State, are guaranteed. The content and limitations of these rights are established by law.

Private property is guaranteed and equally protected by law, irrespective of its holder. Foreign citizens and stateless persons may acquire the right of private ownership of land only under the conditions resulting from Romania's accession to the European Union and from other international treaties to which Romania is a party, on a reciprocal basis, under the conditions provided by organic law, as well as by legal inheritance.
No one may be expropriated except for a cause of public utility, established according to law, with just and prior compensation.

Nationalization or any other measures of forced transfer of assets to public ownership based on the social, ethnic, religious, political or other discriminatory affiliation of the holders are prohibited.

For works of general interest, the public authority may use the subsoil of any immovable property, with the obligation to compensate the owner for damages caused to the soil, plantations or constructions, as well as for other damages imputable to the authority.
The compensations provided for in paragraphs (3) and (5) shall be established by mutual agreement with the owner or, in case of disagreement, by a court of law.

The right of property obliges to the observance of duties regarding environmental protection and ensuring good neighborliness, as well as to the observance of other duties which, according to law or custom, fall upon the owner.
Assets lawfully acquired may not be confiscated. The lawful nature of the acquisition is presumed.
Goods intended for, used in or resulting from offenses or contraventions may be confiscated only under the conditions provided by law.

2003:45.contentEn: Free access of persons to an economic activity, free enterprise, and their exercise under the law are guaranteed.

content.text: Free access of persons to an economic activity, free enterprise, and their exercise under the law are guaranteed.

2003:46.contentEn: The right to inheritance is guaranteed.

content.text: The right to inheritance is guaranteed.

2003:47.contentEn:
1. The State is obliged to take measures of economic development and social protection, of such a nature as to ensure citizens a decent standard of living.
2. Citizens have the right to a pension, paid maternity leave, medical assistance in state medical units, unemployment benefits and other forms of public or private social insurance, provided by law. Citizens also have the right to social assistance measures, according to the law.

content.text: The State is obliged to take measures of economic development and social protection, of such a nature as to ensure citizens a decent standard of living.

Citizens have the right to a pension, paid maternity leave, medical assistance in state medical units, unemployment benefits and other forms of public or private social insurance, provided by law. Citizens also have the right to social assistance measures, according to the law.

2003:48.contentEn:
1. The family is founded on the freely consented marriage between spouses, on their equality and on the right and duty of parents to ensure the upbringing, education and instruction of children.
2. The conditions for entering into, dissolution and nullity of marriage shall be established by law. Religious marriage may be celebrated only after civil marriage.
3. Children born out of wedlock are equal before the law with those born in wedlock.

content.text: The family is founded on the freely consented marriage between spouses, on their equality and on the right and duty of parents to ensure the upbringing, education and instruction of children.
The conditions for entering into, dissolution and nullity of marriage shall be established by law. Religious marriage may be celebrated only after civil marriage.
Children born out of wedlock are equal before the law with those born in wedlock.

2003:49.titleEn: Protection of Children and Young People
2003:49.contentEn:
1. Children and young people enjoy a special protection and assistance regime in the realization of their rights.
2. The State grants allowances for children and aid for the care of sick or disabled children. Other forms of social protection for children and young people shall be established by law.
3. The exploitation of minors, their use in activities that would harm their health, morality or endanger their life or normal development are prohibited.
4. Minors under the age of 15 may not be employed as employees.
5. Public authorities have the obligation to contribute to ensuring the conditions for the free participation of young people in the political, social, economic, cultural and sports life of the country.

content.text: Children and young people enjoy a special protection and assistance regime in the realization of their rights.
The State grants allowances for children and aid for the care of sick or disabled children. Other forms of social protection for children and young people shall be established by law.
The exploitation of minors, their use in activities that would harm their health, morality or endanger their life or normal development are prohibited.
Minors under the age of 15 may not be employed as employees.
Public authorities have the obligation to contribute to ensuring the conditions for the free participation of young people in the political, social, economic, cultural and sports life of the country.

2003:50.contentEn: Persons with disabilities shall enjoy special protection. The State shall ensure the implementation of a national policy of equal opportunities, prevention and treatment of disability, in order to ensure the effective participation of persons with disabilities in community life, while respecting the rights and duties of parents and guardians.

content.text: Persons with disabilities shall enjoy special protection. The State shall ensure the implementation of a national policy of equal opportunities, prevention and treatment of disability, in order to ensure the effective participation of persons with disabilities in community life, while respecting the rights and duties of parents and guardians.

2003:51.titleEn: Respect for the Constitution and the Laws
2003:51.contentEn: Respect for the Constitution, its supremacy and the laws is mandatory.

content.text: Respect for the Constitution, its supremacy and the laws is mandatory.

2003:52.titleEn: Right of the Person Aggrieved by a Public Authority
2003:52.contentEn:
1. A person aggrieved in his/her right or legitimate interest by a public authority, through an administrative act or by failure to resolve a request within the legal term, shall be entitled to obtain the recognition of the claimed right or legitimate interest, the annulment of the act and the reparation of the damage.
2. The conditions and limits for the exercise of this right shall be established by organic law.
3. The State shall be liable for damages caused by judicial errors. The liability of the State shall be established under the conditions provided by law and shall not eliminate the liability of magistrates who have exercised their function in bad faith or with gross negligence.

content.text: A person aggrieved in his/her right or legitimate interest by a public authority, through an administrative act or by failure to resolve a request within the legal term, shall be entitled to obtain the recognition of the claimed right or legitimate interest, the annulment of the act and the reparation of the damage.
The conditions and limits for the exercise of this right shall be established by organic law.

The State shall be liable for damages caused by judicial errors. The liability of the State shall be established under the conditions provided by law and shall not eliminate the liability of magistrates who have exercised their function in bad faith or with gross negligence.

2003:53.titleEn: Restriction on the Exercise of Certain Rights or Freedoms
2003:53.contentEn:
1. The exercise of certain rights or freedoms may be restricted only by law and only if necessary, as the case may be, for: the defence of national security, public order, health or morals, the rights and freedoms of citizens; the conduct of criminal investigations; the prevention of the consequences of a natural calamity, disaster or particularly serious catastrophe.
2. The restriction may be ordered only if it is necessary in a democratic society. The measure must be proportionate to the situation that determined it, be applied in a non-discriminatory manner and without prejudice to the existence of the right or freedom.

content.text: The exercise of certain rights or freedoms may be restricted only by law and only if necessary, as the case may be, for: the defence of national security, public order, health or morals, the rights and freedoms of citizens; the conduct of criminal investigations; the prevention of the consequences of a natural calamity, disaster or particularly serious catastrophe.

The restriction may be ordered only if it is necessary in a democratic society. The measure must be proportionate to the situation that determined it, be applied in a non-discriminatory manner and without prejudice to the existence of the right or freedom.

2003:54.contentEn:
1. Loyalty to the country is sacred.
2. Citizens entrusted with public functions, as well as military personnel, shall be responsible for the faithful fulfilment of their obligations and, to this end, shall take the oath required by law.

content.text: Loyalty to the country is sacred.
Citizens entrusted with public functions, as well as military personnel, shall be responsible for the faithful fulfilment of their obligations and, to this end, shall take the oath required by law.

2003:55.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall have the right and obligation to defend Romania.
2. The conditions regarding the fulfilment of military duties shall be established by organic law.
3. Citizens may be conscripted from the age of 20 up to the age of 35, with the exception of volunteers, under the conditions provided by organic law.

content.text: Citizens shall have the right and obligation to defend Romania.
The conditions regarding the fulfilment of military duties shall be established by organic law.
Citizens may be conscripted from the age of 20 up to the age of 35, with the exception of volunteers, under the conditions provided by organic law.

2003:56.contentEn:
1. Citizens shall have the obligation to contribute, through taxes and duties, to public expenditure.
2. The legal system of taxation must ensure the fair allocation of tax burdens.
3. Any other services are prohibited, except those established by law, in exceptional situations.

content.text: Citizens shall have the obligation to contribute, through taxes and duties, to public expenditure.
The legal system of taxation must ensure the fair allocation of tax burdens.
Any other services are prohibited, except those established by law, in exceptional situations.

2003:57.contentEn: Romanian citizens, foreign citizens, and stateless persons shall exercise their constitutional rights and freedoms in good faith, without infringing on the rights and freedoms of others.

content.text: Romanian citizens, foreign citizens, and stateless persons shall exercise their constitutional rights and freedoms in good faith, without infringing on the rights and freedoms of others.

2003:58.contentEn:
1. The People's Advocate shall be appointed for a term of 5 years to defend the rights and freedoms of natural persons. The deputies of the People's Advocate shall be specialized in fields of activity.
2. The People's Advocate and his/her deputies may not hold any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.
3. The organization and functioning of the institution of the People's Advocate shall be established by organic law.

content.text: The People's Advocate shall be appointed for a term of 5 years to defend the rights and freedoms of natural persons. The deputies of the People's Advocate shall be specialized in fields of activity.
The People's Advocate and his/her deputies may not hold any other public or private office, with the exception of teaching positions in higher education.
The organization and functioning of the institution of the People's Advocate shall be established by organic law.

2003:59.contentEn:
1. The People's Advocate shall exercise his/her attributions ex officio or at the request of persons injured in their rights and freedoms, within the limits established by law.
2. Public authorities are obliged to provide the People's Advocate with the necessary support in the exercise of his/her attributions.

content.text: The People's Advocate shall exercise his/her attributions ex officio or at the request of persons injured in their rights and freedoms, within the limits established by law.
Public authorities are obliged to provide the People's Advocate with the necessary support in the exercise of his/her attributions.

2003:60.contentEn: The People's Advocate shall present reports to the two Chambers of Parliament, annually or at their request. The reports may contain recommendations regarding legislation or measures of another nature, for the protection of the rights and freedoms of citizens.

content.text: The People's Advocate shall present reports to the two Chambers of Parliament, annually or at their request. The reports may contain recommendations regarding legislation or measures of another nature, for the protection of the rights and freedoms of citizens.

2003:61.contentEn:
1. Parliament is the supreme representative body of the Romanian people and the sole legislative authority of the country.
2. Parliament shall be composed of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate.

content.text: Parliament is the supreme representative body of the Romanian people and the sole legislative authority of the country.
Parliament shall be composed of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate.

2003:62.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret, and freely expressed vote, according to the electoral law.
2. Organizations of citizens belonging to national minorities, which do not obtain in the elections the number of votes to be represented in Parliament, shall be entitled to one seat of deputy each, under the conditions of the electoral law. Citizens of a national minority may be represented only by one organization.
3. The number of deputies and senators shall be established by the electoral law, in proportion to the country's population.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret, and freely expressed vote, according to the electoral law.

Organizations of citizens belonging to national minorities, which do not obtain in the elections the number of votes to be represented in Parliament, shall be entitled to one seat of deputy each, under the conditions of the electoral law. Citizens of a national minority may be represented only by one organization.
The number of deputies and senators shall be established by the electoral law, in proportion to the country's population.

2003:63.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected for a term of 4 years, which shall be extended de jure in a state of mobilization, war, siege, or emergency, until their cessation.
2. Elections for the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall take place no later than 3 months from the expiration of the mandate or from the dissolution of Parliament.
3. The newly elected Parliament shall convene, at the convocation of the President of Romania, no later than 20 days from the elections.
4. The mandate of the Chambers shall be extended until the legal meeting of the new Parliament. During this period, the Constitution may not be revised, and organic laws may not be adopted, amended, or repealed.
5. Draft laws or legislative proposals on the agenda of the previous Parliament shall continue their procedure in the new Parliament.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall be elected for a term of 4 years, which shall be extended de jure in a state of mobilization, war, siege, or emergency, until their cessation.
Elections for the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall take place no later than 3 months from the expiration of the mandate or from the dissolution of Parliament.
The newly elected Parliament shall convene, at the convocation of the President of Romania, no later than 20 days from the elections.

The mandate of the Chambers shall be extended until the legal meeting of the new Parliament. During this period, the Constitution may not be revised, and organic laws may not be adopted, amended, or repealed.
Draft laws or legislative proposals on the agenda of the previous Parliament shall continue their procedure in the new Parliament.

2003:64.contentEn:
1. The organization and functioning of each Chamber shall be established by its own regulation. The financial resources of the Chambers shall be provided for in the budgets approved by them.
2. Each Chamber shall elect a standing bureau. The President of the Chamber of Deputies and the President of the Senate shall be elected for the duration of the Chambers' mandate. The other members of the standing bureaus shall be elected at the beginning of each session. Members of the standing bureaus may be removed before the expiration of their mandate.
3. Deputies and senators may organize themselves into parliamentary groups, according to the regulation of each Chamber.
4. Each Chamber shall establish standing committees and may establish inquiry committees or other special committees. The Chambers may establish joint committees.
5. The standing bureaus and parliamentary committees shall be composed according to the political configuration of each Chamber.

content.text: The organization and functioning of each Chamber shall be established by its own regulation. The financial resources of the Chambers shall be provided for in the budgets approved by them.

Each Chamber shall elect a standing bureau. The President of the Chamber of Deputies and the President of the Senate shall be elected for the duration of the Chambers' mandate. The other members of the standing bureaus shall be elected at the beginning of each session. Members of the standing bureaus may be removed before the expiration of their mandate.
Deputies and senators may organize themselves into parliamentary groups, according to the regulation of each Chamber.
Each Chamber shall establish standing committees and may establish inquiry committees or other special committees. The Chambers may establish joint committees.
The standing bureaus and parliamentary committees shall be composed according to the political configuration of each Chamber.
The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall work in separate sittings.
The Chambers shall also carry out their proceedings in joint sittings, according to a regulation adopted by the majority vote of the deputies and senators, for:
a) receiving the message of the President of Romania;
b) approving the state budget and the state social security budget;
c) declaring total or partial mobilization;
d) declaring the state of war;
e) suspending or ceasing military hostilities;
f) approving the national defence strategy of the country;
g) examining the reports of the Supreme Council of National Defence;
h) appointing, upon the proposal of the President of Romania, the directors of the intelligence services and exercising control over the activity of these services;
i) appointing the People's Advocate;
j) establishing the status of deputies and senators, establishing their allowance and other rights thereof;
k) fulfilling other attributions which, according to the Constitution or the regulation, are exercised in joint sitting.

2003:66.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall meet in two ordinary sessions per year. The first session shall begin in February and may not exceed the end of June. The second session shall begin in September and may not exceed the end of December.
2. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall also meet in extraordinary sessions, at the request of the President of Romania, of the standing bureau of each Chamber or of at least one third of the number of deputies or senators.
3. The summoning of the Chambers shall be made by their presidents.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall meet in two ordinary sessions per year. The first session shall begin in February and may not exceed the end of June. The second session shall begin in September and may not exceed the end of December.

The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall also meet in extraordinary sessions, at the request of the President of Romania, of the standing bureau of each Chamber or of at least one third of the number of deputies or senators.
The summoning of the Chambers shall be made by their presidents.

2003:67.contentEn: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall adopt laws, decisions and motions, in the presence of the majority of the members.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate shall adopt laws, decisions and motions, in the presence of the majority of the members.

2003:68.contentEn:
1. The sittings of the two Chambers shall be public.
2. The Chambers may decide that certain sittings be secret.

content.text: The sittings of the two Chambers shall be public.
The Chambers may decide that certain sittings be secret.

2003:69.contentEn:
1. In the exercise of their mandate, the deputies and senators shall be in the service of the people.
2. Any imperative mandate shall be null.

content.text: In the exercise of their mandate, the deputies and senators shall be in the service of the people.
Any imperative mandate shall be null.

2003:70.contentEn:
1. The deputies and senators shall enter into the exercise of their mandate on the date of the legal meeting of the Chamber they are part of, under the condition of the validation of the election and the taking of the oath. The oath shall be established by organic law.
2. The capacity as deputy or senator shall cease on the date of the legal meeting of the newly elected Chambers or in case of resignation, loss of electoral rights, incompatibility or death.

content.text: The deputies and senators shall enter into the exercise of their mandate on the date of the legal meeting of the Chamber they are part of, under the condition of the validation of the election and the taking of the oath. The oath shall be established by organic law.
The capacity as deputy or senator shall cease on the date of the legal meeting of the newly elected Chambers or in case of resignation, loss of electoral rights, incompatibility or death.

2003:71.contentEn:
1. No one may be, at the same time, a deputy and a senator.
2. The capacity as deputy or senator is incompatible with the exercise of any public office of authority, with the exception of that of member of the Government.
3. Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

content.text: No one may be, at the same time, a deputy and a senator.
The capacity as deputy or senator is incompatible with the exercise of any public office of authority, with the exception of that of member of the Government.
Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

2003:72.contentEn:
1. Deputies and senators may not be held legally liable for the votes cast or for the political opinions expressed in the exercise of their mandate.
2. Deputies and senators may be prosecuted and sent to court on criminal charges for facts that are not related to the votes cast or to the political opinions expressed in the exercise of their mandate, but they may not be searched, detained or arrested without the approval of the Chamber they are part of, after hearing them. The prosecution and sending to court on criminal charges may be carried out only by the Prosecutor's Office attached to the High Court of Cassation and Justice. The jurisdiction to adjudicate belongs to the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
3. In case of flagrant offence, the deputies or senators may be detained and subjected to search. The Minister of Justice shall immediately inform the president of the Chamber of the detention and search. In the event that the notified Chamber finds that there is no ground for detention, it shall immediately order the revocation of this measure.

content.text: Deputies and senators may not be held legally liable for the votes cast or for the political opinions expressed in the exercise of their mandate.

Deputies and senators may be prosecuted and sent to court on criminal charges for facts that are not related to the votes cast or to the political opinions expressed in the exercise of their mandate, but they may not be searched, detained or arrested without the approval of the Chamber they are part of, after hearing them. The prosecution and sending to court on criminal charges may be carried out only by the Prosecutor's Office attached to the High Court of Cassation and Justice. The jurisdiction to adjudicate belongs to the High Court of Cassation and Justice.

In case of flagrant offence, the deputies or senators may be detained and subjected to search. The Minister of Justice shall immediately inform the president of the Chamber of the detention and search. In the event that the notified Chamber finds that there is no ground for detention, it shall immediately order the revocation of this measure.
Parliament shall pass constitutional laws, organic laws, and ordinary laws.
Constitutional laws are those revising the Constitution.
Organic laws shall regulate:
a) the electoral system; the organisation and operation of the Permanent Electoral Authority;
b) the organisation, operation, and financing of political parties;
c) the status of deputies and senators, the establishment of their allowance and other rights thereof;
d) the organisation and conduct of referendums;
e) the organisation of the Government and of the Supreme Council of National Defence;
f) the regime of the state of partial or total mobilisation of the armed forces and the state of war;
g) the regime of the state of siege and the state of emergency;
h) offences, penalties, and the regime for the execution thereof;
i) the granting of amnesty or collective pardon;
j) the status of civil servants;
k) administrative litigation;
l) the organisation and operation of the Superior Council of Magistracy, of the courts of law, of the Public Ministry, and of the Court of Accounts;
m) the general legal regime of property and inheritance;
n) the general organisation of education;
o) the organisation of local public administration, of the territory, as well as the general regime regarding local autonomy;
p) the general regime regarding labour relations, trade unions, employers' organisations, and social protection;
r) the status of national minorities in Romania;
s) the general regime of religious denominations;
t) other fields for which the Constitution provides for the adoption of organic laws.

2003:74.contentEn:
1. The legislative initiative shall lie, as the case may be, with the Government, deputies, senators, or a number of at least 100,000 citizens with the right to vote. The citizens exercising their right to legislative initiative must come from at least one quarter of the country's counties, and in each of these counties, respectively in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 5,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.
2. Fiscal matters, international issues, amnesty, and pardon may not be the subject of citizens' legislative initiative.
3. The Government shall exercise its legislative initiative by transmitting the draft law to the Chamber competent to adopt it, as the first notified Chamber.
4. Deputies, senators, and citizens exercising the right to legislative initiative may submit legislative proposals only in the form required for draft laws.
5. Legislative proposals shall be submitted for debate first to the Chamber competent to adopt them, as the first notified Chamber.

content.text: The legislative initiative shall lie, as the case may be, with the Government, deputies, senators, or a number of at least 100,000 citizens with the right to vote. The citizens exercising their right to legislative initiative must come from at least one quarter of the country's counties, and in each of these counties, respectively in the Municipality of Bucharest, at least 5,000 signatures must be registered in support of this initiative.
Fiscal matters, international issues, amnesty, and pardon may not be the subject of citizens' legislative initiative.
The Government shall exercise its legislative initiative by transmitting the draft law to the Chamber competent to adopt it, as the first notified Chamber.
Deputies, senators, and citizens exercising the right to legislative initiative may submit legislative proposals only in the form required for draft laws.
Legislative proposals shall be submitted for debate first to the Chamber competent to adopt them, as the first notified Chamber.

2003:75.contentEn:
1. Draft laws and legislative proposals for the ratification of treaties or other international agreements and legislative measures resulting from the application of such treaties or agreements, as well as draft organic laws provided for in [Articolul 31 paragraph (5)](#articolul-31-dreptul-la-informație), [Articolul 40 paragraph (3)](#articolul-40-dreptul-de-asociere), [Articolul 55 paragraph (2)](#articolul-55-apărarea-țării), [Articolul 58 paragraph (3)](#articolul-58-numirea-și-rolul), [Articolul 73 paragraph (3)](#articolul-73-categorii-de-legi) letters e) , k) , l) , n) , o), [Articolul 79 paragraph (2)](#articolul-79-consiliul-legislativ), [Articolul 102 paragraph (3)](#articolul-102-rolul-și-structura), [Articolul 105 paragraph (2)](#articolul-105-incompatibilități), [Articolul 117 paragraph (3)](#articolul-117-înființarea), [Articolul 118 paragraphs (2) and (3)](#articolul-118-forțele-armate), [Articolul 120 paragraph (2)](#articolul-120-principii-de-bază), [Articolul 126 paragraphs (4) and (5)](#articolul-126-instanțele-judecătorești) and [Articolul 142 paragraph (5)](#articolul-142-structura), shall be submitted to the Chamber of Deputies for debate and adoption, as the first notified Chamber. The other draft laws or legislative proposals shall be submitted to the Senate for debate and adoption, as the first notified Chamber.
2. The first notified Chamber shall pronounce within 45 days. For codes and other laws of particular complexity, the term shall be 60 days. If these terms are exceeded, the draft laws or legislative proposals shall be deemed to have been adopted.
3. After adoption or rejection by the first notified Chamber, the draft or legislative proposal shall be sent to the other Chamber, which shall decide definitively.
4. If the first notified Chamber adopts a provision which, according to paragraph (1), falls within its decision-making competence, the provision shall be definitively adopted if the second Chamber also agrees. Otherwise, only for that provision, the law shall be returned to the first notified Chamber, which shall decide definitively in an emergency procedure.
5. The provisions of paragraph (4) regarding the return of the law shall apply mutatis mutandis if the decision-making Chamber adopts a provision for which the decision-making competence belongs to the first Chamber.

content.text: Draft laws and legislative proposals for the ratification of treaties or other international agreements and legislative measures resulting from the application of such treaties or agreements, as well as draft organic laws provided for in [Articolul 31 paragraph (5)](#articolul-31-dreptul-la-informație), [Articolul 40 paragraph (3)](#articolul-40-dreptul-de-asociere), [Articolul 55 paragraph (2)](#articolul-55-apărarea-țării), [Articolul 58 paragraph (3)](#articolul-58-numirea-și-rolul), [Articolul 73 paragraph (3)](#articolul-73-categorii-de-legi) letters e) , k) , l) , n) , o), [Articolul 79 paragraph (2)](#articolul-79-consiliul-legislativ), [Articolul 102 paragraph (3)](#articolul-102-rolul-și-structura), [Articolul 105 paragraph (2)](#articolul-105-incompatibilități), [Articolul 117 paragraph (3)](#articolul-117-înființarea), [Articolul 118 paragraphs (2) and (3)](#articolul-118-forțele-armate), [Articolul 120 paragraph (2)](#articolul-120-principii-de-bază), [Articolul 126 paragraphs (4) and (5)](#articolul-126-instanțele-judecătorești) and [Articolul 142 paragraph (5)](#articolul-142-structura), shall be submitted to the Chamber of Deputies for debate and adoption, as the first notified Chamber. The other draft laws or legislative proposals shall be submitted to the Senate for debate and adoption, as the first notified Chamber.

The first notified Chamber shall pronounce within 45 days. For codes and other laws of particular complexity, the term shall be 60 days. If these terms are exceeded, the draft laws or legislative proposals shall be deemed to have been adopted.
After adoption or rejection by the first notified Chamber, the draft or legislative proposal shall be sent to the other Chamber, which shall decide definitively.

If the first notified Chamber adopts a provision which, according to paragraph (1), falls within its decision-making competence, the provision shall be definitively adopted if the second Chamber also agrees. Otherwise, only for that provision, the law shall be returned to the first notified Chamber, which shall decide definitively in an emergency procedure.

The provisions of paragraph (4) regarding the return of the law shall apply mutatis mutandis if the decision-making Chamber adopts a provision for which the decision-making competence belongs to the first Chamber.

2003:76.contentEn:
1. Organic laws and decisions regarding the regulations of the Houses shall be adopted by the vote of the majority of the members of each House.
2. Ordinary laws and decisions shall be adopted by the vote of the majority of the members present in each House.
3. At the request of the Government or on its own initiative, Parliament may adopt draft laws or legislative proposals under an emergency procedure, established according to the regulations of each House.

content.text: Organic laws and decisions regarding the regulations of the Houses shall be adopted by the vote of the majority of the members of each House.
Ordinary laws and decisions shall be adopted by the vote of the majority of the members present in each House.

At the request of the Government or on its own initiative, Parliament may adopt draft laws or legislative proposals under an emergency procedure, established according to the regulations of each House.

2003:77.contentEn:
1. The law shall be sent to the President of Romania for promulgation. The promulgation of the law shall take place within a maximum of 20 days from its receipt.
2. Before promulgation, the President may request Parliament, only once, to re-examine the law.
3. If the President has requested the re-examination of the law or if the verification of its constitutionality has been requested, the promulgation of the law shall take place within a maximum of 10 days from the receipt of the law adopted after re-examination or from the receipt of the decision of the Constitutional Court, by which its constitutionality has been confirmed.

content.text: The law shall be sent to the President of Romania for promulgation. The promulgation of the law shall take place within a maximum of 20 days from its receipt.
Before promulgation, the President may request Parliament, only once, to re-examine the law.

If the President has requested the re-examination of the law or if the verification of its constitutionality has been requested, the promulgation of the law shall take place within a maximum of 10 days from the receipt of the law adopted after re-examination or from the receipt of the decision of the Constitutional Court, by which its constitutionality has been confirmed.

2003:78.titleEn: Entry into Force of the Law
2003:78.contentEn: The law shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania and shall enter into force 3 days from the date of publication or on a later date provided for in its text.

content.text: The law shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania and shall enter into force 3 days from the date of publication or on a later date provided for in its text.

2003:79.contentEn:
1. The Legislative Council is a specialized consultative body of Parliament, which advises on draft normative acts for the purpose of systematizing, unifying and coordinating the entire legislation. It keeps the official record of the legislation of Romania.
2. The establishment, organization and functioning of the Legislative Council shall be established by organic law.

content.text: The Legislative Council is a specialized consultative body of Parliament, which advises on draft normative acts for the purpose of systematizing, unifying and coordinating the entire legislation. It keeps the official record of the legislation of Romania.
The establishment, organization and functioning of the Legislative Council shall be established by organic law.

2003:80.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania represents the Romanian State and is the guarantor of national independence, of national unity and of the territorial integrity of the Country.
2. The President of Romania shall ensure the observance of the Constitution and the proper functioning of public authorities. To this end, the President shall exercise a mediation office between the powers of the State, as well as between the State and society.

content.text: The President of Romania represents the Romanian State and is the guarantor of national independence, of national unity and of the territorial integrity of the Country.

The President of Romania shall ensure the observance of the Constitution and the proper functioning of public authorities. To this end, the President shall exercise a mediation office between the powers of the State, as well as between the State and society.

2003:81.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret and free suffrage.
2. The candidate who, in the first ballot, obtains a majority of the votes of the voters entered on the electoral rolls shall be declared elected.
3. If none of the candidates has obtained such majority, a second ballot shall be held between the first two candidates in the order of the number of votes cast in the first ballot. The candidate having the greatest number of votes shall be declared elected.
4. No one may hold the office of President of Romania for more than two terms of office, even if these are consecutive.

content.text: The President of Romania shall be elected by universal, equal, direct, secret and free suffrage.
The candidate who, in the first ballot, obtains a majority of the votes of the voters entered on the electoral rolls shall be declared elected.

If none of the candidates has obtained such majority, a second ballot shall be held between the first two candidates in the order of the number of votes cast in the first ballot. The candidate having the greatest number of votes shall be declared elected.
No one may hold the office of President of Romania for more than two terms of office, even if these are consecutive.

2003:82.titleEn: Validation of the Mandate and Swearing in
2003:82.contentEn:
1. The election returns for the office of President of Romania shall be validated by the Constitutional Court.
2. The candidate whose election has been validated shall take before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, the following oath: "I solemnly swear that I will dedicate all my strength and utmost ability to the spiritual and material welfare of the Romanian people, to abide by the Constitution and laws of the Country, to defend democracy, the fundamental rights and freedoms of the citizens, Romania's sovereignty, independence, unity and territorial integrity. So help me God!".

content.text: The election returns for the office of President of Romania shall be validated by the Constitutional Court.

The candidate whose election has been validated shall take before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, the following oath: "I solemnly swear that I will dedicate all my strength and utmost ability to the spiritual and material welfare of the Romanian people, to abide by the Constitution and laws of the Country, to defend democracy, the fundamental rights and freedoms of the citizens, Romania's sovereignty, independence, unity and territorial integrity. So help me God!".

2003:83.contentEn:
1. The term of office of the President of Romania shall be five years, and shall commence on the date the oath is taken.
2. The President of Romania shall exercise his office until the new President takes the oath.
3. The term of office of the President of Romania may be extended by an organic law, in the event of war or disaster.

content.text: The term of office of the President of Romania shall be five years, and shall commence on the date the oath is taken.
The President of Romania shall exercise his office until the new President takes the oath.
The term of office of the President of Romania may be extended by an organic law, in the event of war or disaster.

2003:84.contentEn:
1. During his term of office, the President of Romania may not be a member of any political party, nor perform any other public or private office.
2. The President of Romania shall enjoy immunity. The provisions of Articolul 72 paragraph (1) shall apply accordingly.

content.text: During his term of office, the President of Romania may not be a member of any political party, nor perform any other public or private office.
The President of Romania shall enjoy immunity. The provisions of Articolul 72 paragraph (1) shall apply accordingly.

2003:85.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister and shall appoint the Government on the basis of the vote of confidence granted by Parliament.
2. In the event of government reshuffle or vacancy of office, the President shall dismiss and appoint, on the proposal of the Prime Minister, some members of the Government.
3. If, through the government reshuffle proposal, the political structure or composition of the Government is changed, the President of Romania shall be able to exercise the power stipulated under paragraph (2) only on the basis of the Parliament's approval, granted on the proposal of the Prime Minister.

content.text: The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister and shall appoint the Government on the basis of the vote of confidence granted by Parliament.
In the event of government reshuffle or vacancy of office, the President shall dismiss and appoint, on the proposal of the Prime Minister, some members of the Government.

If, through the government reshuffle proposal, the political structure or composition of the Government is changed, the President of Romania shall be able to exercise the power stipulated under paragraph (2) only on the basis of the Parliament's approval, granted on the proposal of the Prime Minister.

2003:86.contentEn: The President of Romania may consult the Government on urgent or particularly important issues.

content.text: The President of Romania may consult the Government on urgent or particularly important issues.

2003:87.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania may take part in the meetings of the Government when debates focus on issues of national interest with regard to foreign policy, defence of the Country, ensurance of public order, and, at the Prime Minister's request, in other instances as well.
2. The President of Romania shall preside over the Government meetings he attends.

content.text: The President of Romania may take part in the meetings of the Government when debates focus on issues of national interest with regard to foreign policy, defence of the Country, ensurance of public order, and, at the Prime Minister's request, in other instances as well.
The President of Romania shall preside over the Government meetings he attends.

2003:88.contentEn: The President of Romania addresses messages to the Parliament regarding the main political issues of the nation.

content.text: The President of Romania addresses messages to the Parliament regarding the main political issues of the nation.

2003:89.contentEn:
1. After consulting the Speakers of both Chambers and the leaders of the parliamentary groups, the President of Romania may dissolve the Parliament if it has not granted a vote of confidence for the formation of the Government within 60 days from the first request and only after rejecting at least two requests for investiture.
2. In the course of one year, the Parliament may be dissolved only once.
3. The Parliament may not be dissolved during the last 6 months of the term of office of the President of Romania, nor during a state of mobilization, war, siege, or emergency.

content.text: After consulting the Speakers of both Chambers and the leaders of the parliamentary groups, the President of Romania may dissolve the Parliament if it has not granted a vote of confidence for the formation of the Government within 60 days from the first request and only after rejecting at least two requests for investiture.
In the course of one year, the Parliament may be dissolved only once.
The Parliament may not be dissolved during the last 6 months of the term of office of the President of Romania, nor during a state of mobilization, war, siege, or emergency.

2003:90.contentEn: The President of Romania, after consulting the Parliament, may request the people to express, by referendum, their will regarding issues of national interest.

content.text: The President of Romania, after consulting the Parliament, may request the people to express, by referendum, their will regarding issues of national interest.

2003:91.titleEn: Powers in the Field of Foreign Policy
2003:91.contentEn:
1. The President concludes international treaties on behalf of Romania, negotiated by the Government, and submits them to the Parliament for ratification within a reasonable time. Other international treaties and agreements are concluded, approved, or ratified according to the procedure established by law.
2. The President, upon the proposal of the Government, accredits and recalls the diplomatic representatives of Romania and approves the establishment, disestablishment, or change of rank of diplomatic missions.
3. The diplomatic representatives of other states are accredited to the President of Romania.

content.text: The President concludes international treaties on behalf of Romania, negotiated by the Government, and submits them to the Parliament for ratification within a reasonable time. Other international treaties and agreements are concluded, approved, or ratified according to the procedure established by law.

The President, upon the proposal of the Government, accredits and recalls the diplomatic representatives of Romania and approves the establishment, disestablishment, or change of rank of diplomatic missions.
The diplomatic representatives of other states are accredited to the President of Romania.

2003:92.titleEn: Powers in the Field of Defence
2003:92.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania is the commander of the armed forces and fulfils the function of president of the Supreme Council of National Defence.
2. He may declare, with the prior approval of the Parliament, the partial or total mobilization of the armed forces. Only in exceptional cases shall the President's decision be subsequently submitted for approval to the Parliament within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.
3. In the event of armed aggression against the country, the President of Romania shall take measures to repel the aggression and shall immediately bring them to the attention of the Parliament by a message. If the Parliament is not in session, it shall convene by right within 24 hours of the outbreak of the aggression.
4. In the event of mobilization or war, the Parliament shall continue its activity throughout the duration of these states, and if it is not in session, it shall convene by right within 24 hours of their declaration.

content.text: The President of Romania is the commander of the armed forces and fulfils the function of president of the Supreme Council of National Defence.

He may declare, with the prior approval of the Parliament, the partial or total mobilization of the armed forces. Only in exceptional cases shall the President's decision be subsequently submitted for approval to the Parliament within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.

In the event of armed aggression against the country, the President of Romania shall take measures to repel the aggression and shall immediately bring them to the attention of the Parliament by a message. If the Parliament is not in session, it shall convene by right within 24 hours of the outbreak of the aggression.

In the event of mobilization or war, the Parliament shall continue its activity throughout the duration of these states, and if it is not in session, it shall convene by right within 24 hours of their declaration.

2003:93.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania institutes, according to the law, a state of siege or a state of emergency throughout the country or in some administrative-territorial units and requests the Parliament to approve the measure adopted within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.
2. If the Parliament is not in session, it shall convene by right within a maximum of 48 hours from the institution of the state of siege or the state of emergency and shall function throughout their duration.

content.text: The President of Romania institutes, according to the law, a state of siege or a state of emergency throughout the country or in some administrative-territorial units and requests the Parliament to approve the measure adopted within a maximum of 5 days from its adoption.

If the Parliament is not in session, it shall convene by right within a maximum of 48 hours from the institution of the state of siege or the state of emergency and shall function throughout their duration.
The President of Romania also performs the following duties:
a) confers decorations and honorary titles;
b) grants the ranks of marshal, general, and admiral;
c) appoints to public offices, under the conditions provided by law;
d) grants individual pardon.

2003:95.contentEn:
1. In the event of committing serious acts by which he violates the provisions of the Constitution, the President of Romania may be suspended from office by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, by the vote of the majority of deputies and senators, after consulting the Constitutional Court. The President may give the Parliament explanations regarding the acts imputed to him.
2. The proposal for suspension from office may be initiated by at least one third of the number of deputies and senators and shall be brought, without delay, to the attention of the President.
3. If the proposal for suspension from office is approved, a referendum for the dismissal of the President shall be organised within a maximum of 30 days.

content.text: In the event of committing serious acts by which he violates the provisions of the Constitution, the President of Romania may be suspended from office by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, by the vote of the majority of deputies and senators, after consulting the Constitutional Court. The President may give the Parliament explanations regarding the acts imputed to him.
The proposal for suspension from office may be initiated by at least one third of the number of deputies and senators and shall be brought, without delay, to the attention of the President.
If the proposal for suspension from office is approved, a referendum for the dismissal of the President shall be organised within a maximum of 30 days.

2003:96.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may decide to impeach the President of Romania for high treason, by a vote of at least two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators.
2. The proposal for impeachment may be initiated by the majority of deputies and senators and shall be immediately brought to the attention of the President of Romania in order to give explanations regarding the deeds imputed to him.
3. From the date of impeachment until the date of dismissal, the President shall be suspended de jure.
4. The High Court of Cassation and Justice shall have jurisdiction to judge. The President shall be dismissed de jure on the date the conviction decision becomes final.

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may decide to impeach the President of Romania for high treason, by a vote of at least two-thirds of the number of deputies and senators.

The proposal for impeachment may be initiated by the majority of deputies and senators and shall be immediately brought to the attention of the President of Romania in order to give explanations regarding the deeds imputed to him.
From the date of impeachment until the date of dismissal, the President shall be suspended de jure.
The High Court of Cassation and Justice shall have jurisdiction to judge. The President shall be dismissed de jure on the date the conviction decision becomes final.

2003:97.contentEn:
1. The office of President of Romania shall become vacant in the event of resignation, dismissal from office, permanent impossibility to exercise the duties, or death.
2. Within 3 months from the date on which the office of President of Romania became vacant, the Government shall organise elections for a new President.

content.text: The office of President of Romania shall become vacant in the event of resignation, dismissal from office, permanent impossibility to exercise the duties, or death.
Within 3 months from the date on which the office of President of Romania became vacant, the Government shall organise elections for a new President.

2003:98.contentEn:
1. If the office of President becomes vacant, or if the President is suspended from office, or if he is temporarily unable to exercise his duties, the interim office shall be ensured, in order, by the President of the Senate or by the President of the Chamber of Deputies.
2. The powers provided for in Articolul 88-90 may not be exercised during the interim presidential office.

content.text: If the office of President becomes vacant, or if the President is suspended from office, or if he is temporarily unable to exercise his duties, the interim office shall be ensured, in order, by the President of the Senate or by the President of the Chamber of Deputies.
The powers provided for in Articolul 88-90 may not be exercised during the interim presidential office.

2003:99.contentEn: If the person ensuring the interim office of President of Romania commits serious acts by which the provisions of the Constitution are violated, [Articolul 95](#articolul-95-suspendarea-din-funcție) and [Articolul 98](#articolul-98-interimatul-funcției) shall apply.

content.text: If the person ensuring the interim office of President of Romania commits serious acts by which the provisions of the Constitution are violated, [Articolul 95](#articolul-95-suspendarea-din-funcție) and [Articolul 98](#articolul-98-interimatul-funcției) shall apply.

2003:100.contentEn:
1. In the exercise of his duties, the President of Romania shall issue decrees which shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decree non-existent.
2. The decrees issued by the President of Romania in the exercise of his duties provided for in [Articolul 91 paragraphs (1) and (2)](#articolul-91-atribuții-în-domeniul-politicii-externe), [Articolul 92 paragraphs (2) and (3)](#articolul-92-atribuții-în-domeniul-apărării), [Articolul 93 paragraph (1)](#articolul-93-măsuri-excepționale) and [Articolul 94 letters a), b) and d)](#articolul-94-alte-atribuții) shall be countersigned by the Prime Minister.

content.text: In the exercise of his duties, the President of Romania shall issue decrees which shall be published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decree non-existent.

The decrees issued by the President of Romania in the exercise of his duties provided for in [Articolul 91 paragraphs (1) and (2)](#articolul-91-atribuții-în-domeniul-politicii-externe), [Articolul 92 paragraphs (2) and (3)](#articolul-92-atribuții-în-domeniul-apărării), [Articolul 93 paragraph (1)](#articolul-93-măsuri-excepționale) and [Articolul 94 letters a), b) and d)](#articolul-94-alte-atribuții) shall be countersigned by the Prime Minister.

2003:101.contentEn: The allowance and other rights of the President of Romania shall be established by law.

content.text: The allowance and other rights of the President of Romania shall be established by law.

2003:102.contentEn:
1. The Government, according to its governing programme accepted by Parliament, shall ensure the implementation of the country's internal and external policy and shall exercise the general management of public administration.
2. In fulfilling its duties, the Government shall cooperate with the interested social bodies.
3. The Government shall be composed of the Prime Minister, ministers and other members established by organic law.

content.text: The Government, according to its governing programme accepted by Parliament, shall ensure the implementation of the country's internal and external policy and shall exercise the general management of public administration.
In fulfilling its duties, the Government shall cooperate with the interested social bodies.
The Government shall be composed of the Prime Minister, ministers and other members established by organic law.

2003:103.contentEn:
1. The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister, following consultation with the party that has an absolute majority in Parliament or, if there is no such majority, with the parties represented in Parliament.
2. The candidate for the office of Prime Minister shall request, within 10 days of designation, a vote of confidence from Parliament on the programme and the entire list of the Government.
3. The programme and the list of the Government shall be debated by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting. Parliament shall grant confidence to the Government by the vote of the majority of deputies and senators.

content.text: The President of Romania shall designate a candidate for the office of Prime Minister, following consultation with the party that has an absolute majority in Parliament or, if there is no such majority, with the parties represented in Parliament.
The candidate for the office of Prime Minister shall request, within 10 days of designation, a vote of confidence from Parliament on the programme and the entire list of the Government.

The programme and the list of the Government shall be debated by the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting. Parliament shall grant confidence to the Government by the vote of the majority of deputies and senators.

2003:104.contentEn:
1. The Prime Minister, ministers, and other members of the Government shall individually take the oath provided for in [articolul 82](#articolul-82-validarea-mandatului-și-depunerea-jurământului) before the President of Romania.
2. The Government as a whole and each member thereof shall exercise their mandate from the date of taking the oath.

content.text: The Prime Minister, ministers, and other members of the Government shall individually take the oath provided for in [articolul 82](#articolul-82-validarea-mandatului-și-depunerea-jurământului) before the President of Romania.
The Government as a whole and each member thereof shall exercise their mandate from the date of taking the oath.

2003:105.contentEn:
1. The office of member of the Government is incompatible with the exercise of any other public office of authority, with the exception of that of deputy or senator. It is also incompatible with the exercise of a salaried professional representation function within organizations with a commercial purpose.
2. Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

content.text: The office of member of the Government is incompatible with the exercise of any other public office of authority, with the exception of that of deputy or senator. It is also incompatible with the exercise of a salaried professional representation function within organizations with a commercial purpose.
Other incompatibilities shall be established by organic law.

2003:106.contentEn: The office of member of the Government shall cease following resignation, dismissal, loss of electoral rights, a state of incompatibility, death, as well as in other cases provided for by law.

content.text: The office of member of the Government shall cease following resignation, dismissal, loss of electoral rights, a state of incompatibility, death, as well as in other cases provided for by law.

2003:107.contentEn:
1. The Prime Minister shall lead the Government and coordinate the activity of its members, respecting the powers vested in them. He shall also present reports and statements to the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate regarding the Government's policy, which shall be debated with priority.
2. The President of Romania may not dismiss the Prime Minister.
3. If the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [articolul 106](#articolul-106-încetarea-funcției-de-membru), with the exception of dismissal, or is unable to exercise his duties, the President of Romania shall designate another member of the Government as interim Prime Minister, to perform the duties of the Prime Minister, until the formation of the new Government. The interim period, during the impossibility of exercising the duties, shall cease if the Prime Minister resumes his activity in the Government.
4. The provisions of paragraph (3) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the other members of the Government, at the proposal of the Prime Minister, for a period of no more than 45 days.

content.text: The Prime Minister shall lead the Government and coordinate the activity of its members, respecting the powers vested in them. He shall also present reports and statements to the Chamber of Deputies or the Senate regarding the Government's policy, which shall be debated with priority.
The President of Romania may not dismiss the Prime Minister.

If the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [articolul 106](#articolul-106-încetarea-funcției-de-membru), with the exception of dismissal, or is unable to exercise his duties, the President of Romania shall designate another member of the Government as interim Prime Minister, to perform the duties of the Prime Minister, until the formation of the new Government. The interim period, during the impossibility of exercising the duties, shall cease if the Prime Minister resumes his activity in the Government.
The provisions of paragraph (3) shall apply mutatis mutandis to the other members of the Government, at the proposal of the Prime Minister, for a period of no more than 45 days.

2003:108.contentEn:
1. The Government shall adopt decisions and ordinances.
2. Decisions shall be issued for the organization of the execution of laws.
3. Ordinances shall be issued on the basis of a special enabling law, within the limits and under the conditions provided for therein.
4. The decisions and ordinances adopted by the Government shall be signed by the Prime Minister, countersigned by the ministers who have the obligation to put them into execution, and published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decision or ordinance non-existent. Decisions of a military nature shall be communicated only to the institutions concerned.

content.text: The Government shall adopt decisions and ordinances.
Decisions shall be issued for the organization of the execution of laws.
Ordinances shall be issued on the basis of a special enabling law, within the limits and under the conditions provided for therein.

The decisions and ordinances adopted by the Government shall be signed by the Prime Minister, countersigned by the ministers who have the obligation to put them into execution, and published in the Official Gazette of Romania. Failure to publish shall render the decision or ordinance non-existent. Decisions of a military nature shall be communicated only to the institutions concerned.

2003:109.titleEn: Responsibility of the Members of the Government
2003:109.contentEn:
1. The Government shall be politically responsible only to Parliament for its entire activity. Each member of the Government shall be politically jointly and severally liable with the other members for the activity of the Government and for its acts.
2. Only the Chamber of Deputies, the Senate, and the President of Romania have the right to request the criminal prosecution of members of the Government for acts committed in the exercise of their office. If criminal prosecution has been requested, the President of Romania may order their suspension from office. The referral to court of a member of the Government shall entail his suspension from office. Jurisdiction lies with the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
3. The cases of liability and the penalties applicable to members of the Government are regulated by a law on ministerial responsibility.

content.text: The Government shall be politically responsible only to Parliament for its entire activity. Each member of the Government shall be politically jointly and severally liable with the other members for the activity of the Government and for its acts.

Only the Chamber of Deputies, the Senate, and the President of Romania have the right to request the criminal prosecution of members of the Government for acts committed in the exercise of their office. If criminal prosecution has been requested, the President of Romania may order their suspension from office. The referral to court of a member of the Government shall entail his suspension from office. Jurisdiction lies with the High Court of Cassation and Justice.
The cases of liability and the penalties applicable to members of the Government are regulated by a law on ministerial responsibility.

2003:110.contentEn:
1. The Government shall exercise its mandate until the date of validation of the general parliamentary elections.
2. The Government shall be dismissed on the date of withdrawal by the Parliament of the confidence granted, or if the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [articolul 106](#articolul-106-încetarea-funcției-de-membru), with the exception of revocation, or is unable to exercise his/her duties for more than 45 days.
3. In the situations provided for in paragraph (2), the provisions of [articolul 103](#articolul-103-investitura) shall apply.
4. The Government whose mandate has ceased according to paragraphs (1) and (2) shall only perform the acts necessary for the administration of public affairs, until the swearing in of the members of the new Government.

content.text: The Government shall exercise its mandate until the date of validation of the general parliamentary elections.

The Government shall be dismissed on the date of withdrawal by the Parliament of the confidence granted, or if the Prime Minister is in one of the situations provided for in [articolul 106](#articolul-106-încetarea-funcției-de-membru), with the exception of revocation, or is unable to exercise his/her duties for more than 45 days.
In the situations provided for in paragraph (2), the provisions of [articolul 103](#articolul-103-investitura) shall apply.

The Government whose mandate has ceased according to paragraphs (1) and (2) shall only perform the acts necessary for the administration of public affairs, until the swearing in of the members of the new Government.

2003:111.contentEn:
1. The Government and the other bodies of public administration shall, within the parliamentary control of their activity, be obliged to present the information and documents requested by the Chamber of Deputies, the Senate, or the parliamentary committees, through their presidents. If a legislative initiative implies the amendment of the provisions of the state budget or of the state social security budget, the request for information shall be mandatory.
2. The members of the Government shall have access to the proceedings of the Parliament. If their presence is requested, their participation shall be mandatory.

content.text: The Government and the other bodies of public administration shall, within the parliamentary control of their activity, be obliged to present the information and documents requested by the Chamber of Deputies, the Senate, or the parliamentary committees, through their presidents. If a legislative initiative implies the amendment of the provisions of the state budget or of the state social security budget, the request for information shall be mandatory.
The members of the Government shall have access to the proceedings of the Parliament. If their presence is requested, their participation shall be mandatory.

2003:112.contentEn:
1. The Government and each of its members shall be obliged to answer the questions or interpellations formulated by the deputies or senators, under the conditions provided for by the regulations of the two Chambers of the Parliament.
2. The Chamber of Deputies or the Senate may adopt a simple motion by which to express its position on a problem of internal or external policy or, as the case may be, on a problem that was the object of an interpellation.

content.text: The Government and each of its members shall be obliged to answer the questions or interpellations formulated by the deputies or senators, under the conditions provided for by the regulations of the two Chambers of the Parliament.

The Chamber of Deputies or the Senate may adopt a simple motion by which to express its position on a problem of internal or external policy or, as the case may be, on a problem that was the object of an interpellation.

2003:113.contentEn:
1. The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may withdraw the confidence granted to the Government by adopting a motion of no confidence, with the vote of the majority of the deputies and senators.
2. The motion of no confidence may be initiated by at least one quarter of the total number of deputies and senators and shall be communicated to the Government on the date of submission.
3. The motion of no confidence shall be debated after 3 days from the date when it was presented in the joint sitting of the two Chambers.
4. If the motion of no confidence has been rejected, the deputies and senators who signed it may no longer initiate, in the same session, a new motion of no confidence, except where the Government assumes responsibility according to [articolul 114](#articolul-114-angajarea-răspunderii-guvernului).

content.text: The Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, may withdraw the confidence granted to the Government by adopting a motion of no confidence, with the vote of the majority of the deputies and senators.
The motion of no confidence may be initiated by at least one quarter of the total number of deputies and senators and shall be communicated to the Government on the date of submission.
The motion of no confidence shall be debated after 3 days from the date when it was presented in the joint sitting of the two Chambers.

If the motion of no confidence has been rejected, the deputies and senators who signed it may no longer initiate, in the same session, a new motion of no confidence, except where the Government assumes responsibility according to [articolul 114](#articolul-114-angajarea-răspunderii-guvernului).

2003:114.titleEn: Assumption of Responsibility by the Government
2003:114.contentEn:
1. The Government may assume responsibility before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, on a program, a declaration of general policy, or a bill.
2. The Government shall be dismissed if a motion of no confidence, submitted within 3 days from the presentation of the program, of the declaration of general policy, or of the bill, has been voted under the conditions of [articolul 113](#articolul-113-moțiunea-de-cenzură).
3. If the Government has not been dismissed according to paragraph (2), the bill presented, amended or completed, as the case may be, with amendments accepted by the Government, shall be deemed adopted, and the application of the program or of the declaration of general policy shall become mandatory for the Government.
4. If the President of Romania requests the re-examination of the law adopted according to paragraph (3), the debate thereof shall take place in the joint sitting of the two Chambers.

content.text: The Government may assume responsibility before the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate, in joint sitting, on a program, a declaration of general policy, or a bill.
